HACCP_term.name: HACCP_term
HACCP_term.title: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) guide food safety term
HACCP_term.description: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) food safety terms; This field accepts terms listed under HACCP guide food safety term (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03530221)

IFSAC_category.name: IFSAC_category
IFSAC_category.title: Interagency Food Safety Analytics Collaboration (IFSAC) category
IFSAC_category.description: The IFSAC food categorization scheme has five distinct levels to which foods can be assigned, depending upon the type of food. First, foods are assigned to one of four food groups (aquatic animals, land animals, plants, and other). Food groups include increasingly specific food categories; dairy, eggs, meat and poultry, and game are in the land animal food group, and the category meat and poultry is further subdivided into more specific categories of meat (beef, pork, other meat) and poultry (chicken, turkey, other poultry). Finally, foods are differentiated by differences in food processing (such as pasteurized fluid dairy products, unpasteurized fluid dairy products, pasteurized solid and semi-solid dairy products, and unpasteurized solid and semi-solid dairy products. An IFSAC food category chart is available from https://www.cdc.gov/foodsafety/ifsac/projects/food-categorization-scheme.html PMID: 28926300

abs_air_humidity.name: abs_air_humidity
abs_air_humidity.title: absolute air humidity
abs_air_humidity.description: Actual mass of water vapor present in the air water vapor mixture.
comments: Can be calculated via mass of water vapor divided by the volume of the air and water vapor mixture.

adapters.name: adapters
adapters.title: adapters
adapters.description: Adapters provide priming sequences for both amplification and sequencing of the sample-library fragments. Both adapters should be reported; in uppercase letters

add_recov_method.name: add_recov_method
add_recov_method.title: secondary and tertiary recovery methods and start date
add_recov_method.description: Additional (i.e. Secondary, tertiary, etc.) recovery methods deployed for increase of hydrocarbon recovery from resource and start date for each one of them. If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

additional_info.name: additional_info
additional_info.title: additional info
additional_info.description: Information that doesn't fit anywhere else. Can also be used to propose new entries for fields with controlled vocabulary

address.name: address
address.title: address
address.description: The street name and building number where the sampling occurred

adj_room.name: adj_room
adj_room.title: adjacent rooms
adj_room.description: List of rooms (room number, room name) immediately adjacent to the sampling room

adjacent_environment.name: adjacent_environment
adjacent_environment.title: environment adjacent to site
adjacent_environment.description: Description of the environmental system or features that are adjacent to the sampling site. This field accepts terms under ecosystem (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_01001110) and human construction (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00000070). Multiple terms can be separated by pipes

aero_struc.name: aero_struc
aero_struc.title: aerospace structure
aero_struc.description: Aerospace structures typically consist of thin plates with stiffeners for the external surfaces, bulkheads and frames to support the shape and fasteners such as welds, rivets, screws and bolts to hold the components together

agrochem_addition.name: agrochem_addition
agrochem_addition.title: history/agrochemical additions
agrochem_addition.description: Addition of fertilizers, pesticides, etc. - amount and time of applications

air_PM_concen.name: air_PM_concen
air_PM_concen.title: air particulate matter concentration
air_PM_concen.description: Concentration of substances that remain suspended in the air, and comprise mixtures of organic and inorganic substances (PM10 and PM2.5); can report multiple PM's by entering numeric values preceded by name of PM

air_flow_impede.name: air_flow_impede
air_flow_impede.title: local air flow impediments
air_flow_impede.description: Presence of objects in the area that would influence or impede air flow through the air filter

air_temp.name: air_temp
air_temp.title: air temperature
air_temp.description: Temperature of the air at the time of sampling

air_temp_regm.name: air_temp_regm
air_temp_regm.title: air temperature regimen
air_temp_regm.description: Information about treatment involving an exposure to varying temperatures; should include the temperature, treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment; can include different temperature regimens

al_sat_meth.name: al_sat_meth
al_sat_meth.title: extreme_unusual_properties/Al saturation method
al_sat_meth.description: Reference or method used in determining Al saturation

alkalinity.name: alkalinity
alkalinity.title: alkalinity
alkalinity.description: Alkalinity, the ability of a solution to neutralize acids to the equivalence point of carbonate or bicarbonate

alt.name: alt
alt.title: altitude
alt.description: Heights of objects such as airplanes, space shuttles, rockets, atmospheric balloons and heights of places such as atmospheric layers and clouds. It is used to measure the height of an object which is above the earth's surface. In this context, the altitude measurement is the vertical distance between the earth's surface above sea level and the sampled position in the air

ammonium.name: ammonium
ammonium.title: ammonium
ammonium.description: Concentration of ammonium in the sample

amniotic_fluid_color.name: amniotic_fluid_color
amniotic_fluid_color.title: amniotic fluid/color
amniotic_fluid_color.description: Specification of the color of the amniotic fluid sample

amount_light.name: amount_light
amount_light.title: amount of light
amount_light.description: The unit of illuminance and luminous emittance, measuring luminous flux per unit area

anim_water_method.name: anim_water_method
anim_water_method.title: animal water delivery method
anim_water_method.description: Description of the equipment or method used to distribute water to livestock. This field accepts termed listed under water delivery equipment (http://opendata.inra.fr/EOL/EOL_0001653). Multiple terms can be separated by pipes

animal_am.name: animal_am
animal_am.title: food animal antimicrobial
animal_am.description: The name(s) (generic or brand) of the antimicrobial(s) given to the food animal within the last 30 days

animal_am_dur.name: animal_am_dur
animal_am_dur.title: food animal antimicrobial duration
animal_am_dur.description: The duration of time (days) that the antimicrobial was administered to the food animal

animal_am_freq.name: animal_am_freq
animal_am_freq.title: food animal antimicrobial frequency
animal_am_freq.description: The frequency per day that the antimicrobial was administered to the food animal

animal_am_route.name: animal_am_route
animal_am_route.title: food animal antimicrobial route of administration
animal_am_route.description: The route by which the antimicrobial is administered into the body of the food animal

animal_am_use.name: animal_am_use
animal_am_use.title: food animal antimicrobial intended use
animal_am_use.description: The prescribed intended use of or the condition treated by the antimicrobial given to the food animal by any route of administration

animal_body_cond.name: animal_body_cond
animal_body_cond.title: food animal body condition
animal_body_cond.description: Body condition scoring is a production management tool used to evaluate overall health and nutritional needs of a food animal. Because there are different scoring systems, this field is restricted to three categories

animal_diet.name: animal_diet
animal_diet.title: food animal source diet
animal_diet.description: If the isolate is from a food animal, the type of diet eaten by the food animal. Please list the main food staple and the setting, if appropriate. For a list of acceptable animal feed terms or categories, please see http://www.feedipedia.org. Multiple terms may apply and can be separated by pipes |Food product for animal covers foods intended for consumption by domesticated animals. Consult http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03309997. If the proper descriptor is not listed please use text to describe the food type. Multiple terms can be separated by one or more pipes. If the proper descriptor is not listed please use text to describe the food product type

animal_feed_equip.name: animal_feed_equip
animal_feed_equip.title: animal feeding equipment
animal_feed_equip.description: Description of the feeding equipment used for livestock. This field accepts terms listed under feed delivery (http://opendata.inra.fr/EOL/EOL_0001757). Multiple terms can be separated by pipes

animal_group_size.name: animal_group_size
animal_group_size.title: food animal group size
animal_group_size.description: The number of food animals of the same species that are maintained together as a unit, i.e. a herd or flock

animal_housing.name: animal_housing
animal_housing.title: animal housing system
animal_housing.description: Description of the housing system of the livestock. This field accepts terms listed under terrestrial management housing system (http://opendata.inra.fr/EOL/EOL_0001605)

animal_intrusion.name: animal_intrusion
animal_intrusion.title: animal intrusion near sample source
animal_intrusion.description: Identification of animals intruding on the sample or sample site including invertebrates (such as pests or pollinators) and vertebrates (such as wildlife or domesticated animals). This field accepts terms under organism (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCIT_C14250). This field also accepts identification numbers from NCBI under https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/taxonomy. Multiple terms can be separated by pipes

animal_sex.name: animal_sex
animal_sex.title: food animal source sex category
animal_sex.description: The sex and reproductive status of the food animal

annot.name: annot
annot.title: annotation
annot.description: Tool used for annotation, or for cases where annotation was provided by a community jamboree or model organism database rather than by a specific submitter

annual_precpt.name: annual_precpt
annual_precpt.title: mean annual precipitation
annual_precpt.description: The average of all annual precipitation values known, or an estimated equivalent value derived by such methods as regional indexes or Isohyetal maps

antibiotic_regm.name: antibiotic_regm
antibiotic_regm.title: antibiotic regimen
antibiotic_regm.description: Information about treatment involving antibiotic administration; should include the name of antibiotic, amount administered, treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment; can include multiple antibiotic regimens

api.name: api
api.title: API gravity
api.description: API gravity is a measure of how heavy or light a petroleum liquid is compared to water (source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/API_gravity) (e.g. 31.1 API)

arch_struc.name: arch_struc
arch_struc.title: architectural structure
arch_struc.description: An architectural structure is a human-made, free-standing, immobile outdoor construction

area_samp_size.name: area_samp_size
area_samp_size.title: area sampled size
area_samp_size.description: The total amount or size (volume (ml), mass (g) or area (m2) ) of sample collected

aromatics_pc.name: aromatics_pc
aromatics_pc.title: aromatics wt%
aromatics_pc.description: Saturate, Aromatic, Resin and Asphaltene (SARA) is an analysis method that divides crude oil components according to their polarizability and polarity. There are three main methods to obtain SARA results. The most popular one is known as the Iatroscan TLC-FID and is referred to as IP-143 (source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saturate,_aromatic,_resin_and_asphaltene)

asphaltenes_pc.name: asphaltenes_pc
asphaltenes_pc.title: asphaltenes wt%
asphaltenes_pc.description: Saturate, Aromatic, Resin and Asphaltene (SARA) is an analysis method that divides crude oil components according to their polarizability and polarity. There are three main methods to obtain SARA results. The most popular one is known as the Iatroscan TLC-FID and is referred to as IP-143 (source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saturate,_aromatic,_resin_and_asphaltene)

assembly_name.name: assembly_name
assembly_name.title: assembly name
assembly_name.description: Name/version of the assembly provided by the submitter that is used in the genome browsers and in the community

assembly_qual.name: assembly_qual
assembly_qual.title: assembly quality
assembly_qual.description: The assembly quality category is based on sets of criteria outlined for each assembly quality category. For MISAG/MIMAG; Finished: Single, validated, contiguous sequence per replicon without gaps or ambiguities with a consensus error rate equivalent to Q50 or better. High Quality Draft:Multiple fragments where gaps span repetitive regions. Presence of the large subunit (LSU) RNA, small subunit (SSU) and the presence of 5.8S rRNA or 5S rRNA depending on whether it is a eukaryotic or prokaryotic genome, respectively. Medium Quality Draft:Many fragments with little to no review of assembly other than reporting of standard assembly statistics. Low Quality Draft:Many fragments with little to no review of assembly other than reporting of standard assembly statistics. Assembly statistics include, but are not limited to total assembly size, number of contigs, contig N50/L50, and maximum contig length. For MIUVIG; Finished: Single, validated, contiguous sequence per replicon without gaps or ambiguities, with extensive manual review and editing to annotate putative gene functions and transcriptional units. High-quality draft genome: One or multiple fragments, totaling 90% of the expected genome or replicon sequence or predicted complete. Genome fragment(s): One or multiple fragments, totalling < 90% of the expected genome or replicon sequence, or for which no genome size could be estimated

assembly_software.name: assembly_software
assembly_software.title: assembly software
assembly_software.description: Tool(s) used for assembly, including version number and parameters

associated_resource.name: associated_resource
associated_resource.title: relevant electronic resources
associated_resource.description: A related resource that is referenced, cited, or otherwise associated to the sequence

association_duration.name: association_duration
association_duration.title: duration of association with the host
association_duration.description: Time spent in host of the symbiotic organism at the time of sampling; relevant scale depends on symbiotic organism and study

avg_dew_point.name: avg_dew_point
avg_dew_point.title: average dew point
avg_dew_point.description: The average of dew point measures taken at the beginning of every hour over a 24 hour period on the sampling day

avg_occup.name: avg_occup
avg_occup.title: average daily occupancy
avg_occup.description: Daily average occupancy of room. Indicate the number of person(s) daily occupying the sampling room

avg_temp.name: avg_temp
avg_temp.title: average temperature
avg_temp.description: The average of temperatures taken at the beginning of every hour over a 24 hour period on the sampling day

bac_prod.name: bac_prod
bac_prod.title: bacterial production
bac_prod.description: Bacterial production in the water column measured by isotope uptake
examples: 5 milligram per cubic meter per day

bac_resp.name: bac_resp
bac_resp.title: bacterial respiration
bac_resp.description: Measurement of bacterial respiration in the water column
examples: 300 micromole oxygen per liter per hour

bacterial_density.name: bacterial_density
bacterial_density.title: bacteria density
bacterial_density.description: Number of bacteria in sample, as defined by bacteria density (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GENEPIO_0000043)
examples: 10 colony forming units per gram dry weight

barometric_press.name: barometric_press
barometric_press.title: barometric pressure
barometric_press.description: Force per unit area exerted against a surface by the weight of air above that surface

bathroom_count.name: bathroom_count
bathroom_count.title: bathroom count
bathroom_count.description: The number of bathrooms in the building

bedroom_count.name: bedroom_count
bedroom_count.title: bedroom count
bedroom_count.description: The number of bedrooms in the building

benzene.name: benzene
benzene.title: benzene
benzene.description: Concentration of benzene in the sample

bin_param.name: bin_param
bin_param.title: binning parameters
bin_param.description: The parameters that have been applied during the extraction of genomes from metagenomic datasets

bin_software.name: bin_software
bin_software.title: binning software
bin_software.description: Tool(s) used for the extraction of genomes from metagenomic datasets, where possible include a product ID (PID) of the tool(s) used

biochem_oxygen_dem.name: biochem_oxygen_dem
biochem_oxygen_dem.title: biochemical oxygen demand
biochem_oxygen_dem.description: Amount of dissolved oxygen needed by aerobic biological organisms in a body of water to break down organic material present in a given water sample at certain temperature over a specific time period

biocide.name: biocide
biocide.title: biocide administration
biocide.description: List of biocides (commercial name of product and supplier) and date of administration

biocide_admin_method.name: biocide_admin_method
biocide_admin_method.title: biocide administration method
biocide_admin_method.description: Method of biocide administration (dose, frequency, duration, time elapsed between last biociding and sampling) (e.g. 150 mg/l; weekly; 4 hr; 3 days)

biocide_used.name: biocide_used
biocide_used.title: biocide
biocide_used.description: Substance intended for preventing, neutralizing, destroying, repelling, or mitigating the effects of any pest or microorganism; that inhibits the growth, reproduction, and activity of organisms, including fungal cells; decreases the number of fungi or pests present; deters microbial growth and degradation of other ingredients in the formulation. Indicate the biocide used on the location where the sample was taken. Multiple terms can be separated by pipes

biomass.name: biomass
biomass.title: biomass
biomass.description: Amount of biomass; should include the name for the part of biomass measured, e.g. Microbial, total. Can include multiple measurements

biotic_regm.name: biotic_regm
biotic_regm.title: biotic regimen
biotic_regm.description: Information about treatment(s) involving use of biotic factors, such as bacteria, viruses or fungi
examples: sample inoculated with Rhizobium spp. Culture

biotic_relationship.name: biotic_relationship
biotic_relationship.title: observed biotic relationship
biotic_relationship.description: Description of relationship(s) between the subject organism and other organism(s) it is associated with. E.g., parasite on species X; mutualist with species Y. The target organism is the subject of the relationship, and the other organism(s) is the object

birth_control.name: birth_control
birth_control.title: birth control
birth_control.description: Specification of birth control medication used

blood_blood_disord.name: blood_blood_disord
blood_blood_disord.title: blood/blood disorder
blood_blood_disord.description: History of blood disorders; can include multiple disorders. The terms should be chosen from the DO (Human Disease Ontology) at http://www.disease-ontology.org, hematopoietic system disease (https://disease-ontology.org/?id=DOID:74)

blood_press_diast.name: blood_press_diast
blood_press_diast.title: host blood pressure diastolic
blood_press_diast.description: Resting diastolic blood pressure, measured as mm mercury

blood_press_syst.name: blood_press_syst
blood_press_syst.title: host blood pressure systolic
blood_press_syst.description: Resting systolic blood pressure, measured as mm mercury

build_docs.name: build_docs
build_docs.title: design, construction, and operation documents
build_docs.description: The building design, construction and operation documents

build_occup_type.name: build_occup_type
build_occup_type.title: building occupancy type
build_occup_type.description: The primary function for which a building or discrete part of a building is intended to be used

building_setting.name: building_setting
building_setting.title: building setting
building_setting.description: A location (geography) where a building is set

built_struc_age.name: built_struc_age
built_struc_age.title: built structure age
built_struc_age.description: The age of the built structure since construction

built_struc_set.name: built_struc_set
built_struc_set.title: built structure setting
built_struc_set.description: The characterization of the location of the built structure as high or low human density

built_struc_type.name: built_struc_type
built_struc_type.title: built structure type
built_struc_type.description: A physical structure that is a body or assemblage of bodies in space to form a system capable of supporting loads

calcium.name: calcium
calcium.title: calcium
calcium.description: Concentration of calcium in the sample

carb_dioxide.name: carb_dioxide
carb_dioxide.title: carbon dioxide
carb_dioxide.description: Carbon dioxide (gas) amount or concentration at the time of sampling

carb_monoxide.name: carb_monoxide
carb_monoxide.title: carbon monoxide
carb_monoxide.description: Carbon monoxide (gas) amount or concentration at the time of sampling

carb_nitro_ratio.name: carb_nitro_ratio
carb_nitro_ratio.title: carbon/nitrogen ratio
carb_nitro_ratio.description: Ratio of amount or concentrations of carbon to nitrogen

ceil_area.name: ceil_area
ceil_area.title: ceiling area
ceil_area.description: The area of the ceiling space within the room

ceil_cond.name: ceil_cond
ceil_cond.title: ceiling condition
ceil_cond.description: The physical condition of the ceiling at the time of sampling; photos or video preferred; use drawings to indicate location of damaged areas

ceil_finish_mat.name: ceil_finish_mat
ceil_finish_mat.title: ceiling finish material
ceil_finish_mat.description: The type of material used to finish a ceiling

ceil_struc.name: ceil_struc
ceil_struc.title: ceiling structure
ceil_struc.description: The construction format of the ceiling

ceil_texture.name: ceil_texture
ceil_texture.title: ceiling texture
ceil_texture.description: The feel, appearance, or consistency of a ceiling surface

ceil_thermal_mass.name: ceil_thermal_mass
ceil_thermal_mass.title: ceiling thermal mass
ceil_thermal_mass.description: The ability of the ceiling to provide inertia against temperature fluctuations. Generally this means concrete that is exposed. A metal deck that supports a concrete slab will act thermally as long as it is exposed to room air flow

ceil_type.name: ceil_type
ceil_type.title: ceiling type
ceil_type.description: The type of ceiling according to the ceiling's appearance or construction

ceil_water_mold.name: ceil_water_mold
ceil_water_mold.title: ceiling signs of water/mold
ceil_water_mold.description: Signs of the presence of mold or mildew on the ceiling

chem_administration.name: chem_administration
chem_administration.title: chemical administration
chem_administration.description: List of chemical compounds administered to the host or site where sampling occurred, and when (e.g. Antibiotics, n fertilizer, air filter); can include multiple compounds. For chemical entities of biological interest ontology (chebi) (v 163), http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/chebi

chem_mutagen.name: chem_mutagen
chem_mutagen.title: chemical mutagen
chem_mutagen.description: Treatment involving use of mutagens; should include the name of mutagen, amount administered, treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment; can include multiple mutagen regimens

chem_oxygen_dem.name: chem_oxygen_dem
chem_oxygen_dem.title: chemical oxygen demand
chem_oxygen_dem.description: A measure of the capacity of water to consume oxygen during the decomposition of organic matter and the oxidation of inorganic chemicals such as ammonia and nitrite

chem_treat_method.name: chem_treat_method
chem_treat_method.title: chemical treatment method
chem_treat_method.description: Method of chemical administration(dose, frequency, duration, time elapsed between administration and sampling) (e.g. 50 mg/l; twice a week; 1 hr; 0 days)

chem_treatment.name: chem_treatment
chem_treatment.title: chemical treatment
chem_treatment.description: List of chemical compounds administered upstream the sampling location where sampling occurred (e.g. Glycols, H2S scavenger, corrosion and scale inhibitors, demulsifiers, and other production chemicals etc.). The commercial name of the product and name of the supplier should be provided. The date of administration should also be included

chimera_check.name: chimera_check
chimera_check.title: chimera check software
chimera_check.description: Tool(s) used for chimera checking, including version number and parameters, to discover and remove chimeric sequences. A chimeric sequence is comprised of two or more phylogenetically distinct parent sequences

chloride.name: chloride
chloride.title: chloride
chloride.description: Concentration of chloride in the sample

climate_environment.name: climate_environment
climate_environment.title: climate environment
climate_environment.description: Treatment involving an exposure to a particular climate; treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment; can include multiple climates

coll_site_geo_feat.name: coll_site_geo_feat
coll_site_geo_feat.title: collection site geographic feature
coll_site_geo_feat.description: Text or terms that describe the geographic feature where the food sample was obtained by the researcher. This field accepts selected terms listed under the following ontologies: anthropogenic geographic feature (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00000002), for example agricultural fairground [ENVO:01000986]; garden [ENVO:00000011} or any of its subclasses; market [ENVO:01000987]; water well [ENVO:01000002]; or human construction (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00000070)

collection_date.name: collection_date
collection_date.title: collection date
collection_date.description: The time of sampling, either as an instance (single point in time) or interval. In case no exact time is available, the date/time can be right truncated i.e. all of these are valid times: 2008-01-23T19:23:10+00:00; 2008-01-23T19:23:10; 2008-01-23; 2008-01; 2008; Except: 2008-01; 2008 all are ISO8601 compliant

compl_appr.name: compl_appr
compl_appr.title: completeness approach
compl_appr.description: The approach used to determine the completeness of a given genomic assembly, which would typically make use of a set of conserved marker genes or a closely related reference genome. For UViG completeness, include reference genome or group used, and contig feature suggesting a complete genome

compl_score.name: compl_score
compl_score.title: completeness score
compl_score.description: Completeness score is typically based on either the fraction of markers found as compared to a database or the percent of a genome found as compared to a closely related reference genome. High Quality Draft: >90%, Medium Quality Draft: >50%, and Low Quality Draft: < 50% should have the indicated completeness scores

compl_software.name: compl_software
compl_software.title: completeness software
compl_software.description: Tools used for completion estimate, i.e. checkm, anvi'o, busco

cons_food_stor_dur.name: cons_food_stor_dur
cons_food_stor_dur.title: food stored by consumer (storage duration)
cons_food_stor_dur.description: The storage duration of the food commodity by the consumer, prior to onset of illness or sample collection. Indicate the timepoint written in ISO 8601 format

cons_food_stor_temp.name: cons_food_stor_temp
cons_food_stor_temp.title: food stored by consumer (storage temperature)
cons_food_stor_temp.description: Temperature at which food commodity was stored by the consumer, prior to onset of illness or sample collection

cons_purch_date.name: cons_purch_date
cons_purch_date.title: purchase date
cons_purch_date.description: The date a food product was purchased by consumer

cons_qty_purchased.name: cons_qty_purchased
cons_qty_purchased.title: quantity purchased
cons_qty_purchased.description: The quantity of food purchased by consumer

contam_score.name: contam_score
contam_score.title: contamination score
contam_score.description: The contamination score is based on the fraction of single-copy genes that are observed more than once in a query genome. The following scores are acceptable for; High Quality Draft: < 5%, Medium Quality Draft: < 10%, Low Quality Draft: < 10%. Contamination must be below 5% for a SAG or MAG to be deposited into any of the public databases

contam_screen_input.name: contam_screen_input
contam_screen_input.title: contamination screening input
contam_screen_input.description: The type of sequence data used as input

contam_screen_param.name: contam_screen_param
contam_screen_param.title: contamination screening parameters
contam_screen_param.description: Specific parameters used in the decontamination sofware, such as reference database, coverage, and kmers. Combinations of these parameters may also be used, i.e. kmer and coverage, or reference database and kmer

crop_rotation.name: crop_rotation
crop_rotation.title: history/crop rotation
crop_rotation.description: Whether or not crop is rotated, and if yes, rotation schedule

crop_yield.name: crop_yield
crop_yield.title: crop yield
crop_yield.description: Amount of crop produced per unit or area of land

cult_isol_date.name: cult_isol_date
cult_isol_date.title: culture isolation date
cult_isol_date.description: The datetime marking the end of a process in which a sample yields a positive result for the target microbial analyte(s) in the form of an isolated colony or colonies

cult_result.name: cult_result
cult_result.title: culture result
cult_result.description: Any result of a bacterial culture experiment reported as a binary assessment such as positive/negative, active/inactive

cult_result_org.name: cult_result_org
cult_result_org.title: culture result organism
cult_result_org.description: Taxonomic information about the cultured organism(s)

cult_root_med.name: cult_root_med
cult_root_med.title: culture rooting medium
cult_root_med.description: Name or reference for the hydroponic or in vitro culture rooting medium; can be the name of a commonly used medium or reference to a specific medium, e.g. Murashige and Skoog medium. If the medium has not been formally published, use the rooting medium descriptors

cult_target.name: cult_target
cult_target.title: culture target microbial analyte
cult_target.description: The target microbial analyte in terms of investigation scope. This field accepts terms under organism (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCIT_C14250). This field also accepts identification numbers from NCBI under https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/taxonomy

cur_vegetation.name: cur_vegetation
cur_vegetation.title: current vegetation
cur_vegetation.description: Vegetation classification from one or more standard classification systems, or agricultural crop

cur_vegetation_meth.name: cur_vegetation_meth
cur_vegetation_meth.title: current vegetation method
cur_vegetation_meth.description: Reference or method used in vegetation classification

date_extr_weath.name: date_extr_weath
date_extr_weath.title: extreme weather date
date_extr_weath.description: Date of unusual weather events that may have affected microbial populations. Multiple terms can be separated by pipes, listed in reverse chronological order

date_last_rain.name: date_last_rain
date_last_rain.title: date last rain
date_last_rain.description: The date of the last time it rained

density.name: density
density.title: density
density.description: Density of the sample, which is its mass per unit volume (aka volumetric mass density)

depos_env.name: depos_env
depos_env.title: depositional environment
depos_env.description: Main depositional environment (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Depositional_environment). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

depth.name: depth
depth.title: depth
depth.description: The vertical distance below local surface. For sediment or soil samples depth is measured from sediment or soil surface, respectively. Depth can be reported as an interval for subsurface samples

dermatology_disord.name: dermatology_disord
dermatology_disord.title: dermatology disorder
dermatology_disord.description: History of dermatology disorders; can include multiple disorders. The terms should be chosen from the DO (Human Disease Ontology) at http://www.disease-ontology.org, skin disease (https://disease-ontology.org/?id=DOID:37)

dew_point.name: dew_point
dew_point.title: dew point
dew_point.description: The temperature to which a given parcel of humid air must be cooled, at constant barometric pressure, for water vapor to condense into water

diet_last_six_month.name: diet_last_six_month
diet_last_six_month.title: major diet change in last six months
diet_last_six_month.description: Specification of major diet changes in the last six months, if yes the change should be specified

dietary_claim_use.name: dietary_claim_use
dietary_claim_use.title: dietary claim or use
dietary_claim_use.description: These descriptors are used either for foods intended for special dietary use as defined in 21 CFR 105 or for foods that have special characteristics indicated in the name or labeling. This field accepts terms listed under dietary claim or use (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03510023). Multiple terms can be separated by one or more pipes, but please consider limiting this list to the most prominent dietary claim or use

diether_lipids.name: diether_lipids
diether_lipids.title: diether lipids
diether_lipids.description: Concentration of diether lipids; can include multiple types of diether lipids

diss_carb_dioxide.name: diss_carb_dioxide
diss_carb_dioxide.title: dissolved carbon dioxide
diss_carb_dioxide.description: Concentration of dissolved carbon dioxide in the sample or liquid portion of the sample

diss_inorg_carb.name: diss_inorg_carb
diss_inorg_carb.title: dissolved inorganic carbon
diss_inorg_carb.description: Dissolved inorganic carbon concentration in the sample, typically measured after filtering the sample using a 0.45 micrometer filter

diss_inorg_phosp.name: diss_inorg_phosp
diss_inorg_phosp.title: dissolved inorganic phosphorus
diss_inorg_phosp.description: Concentration of dissolved inorganic phosphorus in the sample

diss_iron.name: diss_iron
diss_iron.title: dissolved iron
diss_iron.description: Concentration of dissolved iron in the sample

diss_org_carb.name: diss_org_carb
diss_org_carb.title: dissolved organic carbon
diss_org_carb.description: Concentration of dissolved organic carbon in the sample, liquid portion of the sample, or aqueous phase of the fluid

diss_org_nitro.name: diss_org_nitro
diss_org_nitro.title: dissolved organic nitrogen
diss_org_nitro.description: Dissolved organic nitrogen concentration measured as; total dissolved nitrogen - NH4 - NO3 - NO2

diss_oxygen_fluid.name: diss_oxygen_fluid
diss_oxygen_fluid.title: dissolved oxygen in fluids
diss_oxygen_fluid.description: Concentration of dissolved oxygen in the oil field produced fluids as it contributes to oxygen-corrosion and microbial activity (e.g. Mic)

door_comp_type.name: door_comp_type
door_comp_type.title: door type, composite
door_comp_type.description: The composite type of the door

door_cond.name: door_cond
door_cond.title: door condition
door_cond.description: The physical condition of the door

door_loc.name: door_loc
door_loc.title: door location
door_loc.description: The relative location of the door in the room

door_mat.name: door_mat
door_mat.title: door material
door_mat.description: The material the door is composed of

door_move.name: door_move
door_move.title: door movement
door_move.description: The type of movement of the door

door_water_mold.name: door_water_mold
door_water_mold.title: door signs of water/mold
door_water_mold.description: Signs of the presence of mold or mildew on a door

down_par.name: down_par
down_par.title: downward PAR
down_par.description: Visible waveband radiance and irradiance measurements in the water column
examples: 28.71 microEinstein per square meter per second

drainage_class.name: drainage_class
drainage_class.title: drainage classification
drainage_class.description: Drainage classification from a standard system such as the USDA system

drawings.name: drawings
drawings.title: drawings
drawings.description: The buildings architectural drawings; if design is chosen, indicate phase-conceptual, schematic, design development, and construction documents

drug_usage.name: drug_usage
drug_usage.title: drug usage
drug_usage.description: Any drug used by subject and the frequency of usage; can include multiple drugs used

efficiency_percent.name: efficiency_percent
efficiency_percent.title: efficiency percent
efficiency_percent.description: Percentage of volatile solids removed from the anaerobic digestor

elev.name: elev
elev.title: elevation
elev.description: Elevation of the sampling site is its height above a fixed reference point, most commonly the mean sea level. Elevation is mainly used when referring to points on the earth's surface, while altitude is used for points above the surface, such as an aircraft in flight or a spacecraft in orbit

elevator.name: elevator
elevator.title: elevator count
elevator.description: The number of elevators within the built structure

emulsions.name: emulsions
emulsions.title: emulsions
emulsions.description: Amount or concentration of substances such as paints, adhesives, mayonnaise, hair colorants, emulsified oils, etc.; can include multiple emulsion types

encoded_traits.name: encoded_traits
encoded_traits.title: encoded traits
encoded_traits.description: Should include key traits like antibiotic resistance or xenobiotic degradation phenotypes for plasmids, converting genes for phage

enrichment_protocol.name: enrichment_protocol
enrichment_protocol.title: enrichment protocol
enrichment_protocol.description: The microbiological workflow or protocol followed to test for the presence or enumeration of the target microbial analyte(s). Please provide a PubMed or DOI reference for published protocols
examples: BAM Chapter 4: Enumeration of Escherichia coli and the Coliform Bacteria

env_broad_scale.name: env_broad_scale
env_broad_scale.title: broad-scale environmental context
env_broad_scale.description: Report the major environmental system the sample or specimen came from. The system(s) identified should have a coarse spatial grain, to provide the general environmental context of where the sampling was done (e.g. in the desert or a rainforest). We recommend using subclasses of EnvO s biome class: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00000428. EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS

env_local_scale.name: env_local_scale
env_local_scale.title: local environmental context
env_local_scale.description: Report the entity or entities which are in the sample or specimen s local vicinity and which you believe have significant causal influences on your sample or specimen. We recommend using EnvO terms which are of smaller spatial grain than your entry for env_broad_scale. Terms, such as anatomical sites, from other OBO Library ontologies which interoperate with EnvO (e.g. UBERON) are accepted in this field. EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS

env_medium.name: env_medium
env_medium.title: environmental medium
env_medium.description: Report the environmental material(s) immediately surrounding the sample or specimen at the time of sampling. We recommend using subclasses of 'environmental material' (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00010483). EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS . Terms from other OBO ontologies are permissible as long as they reference mass/volume nouns (e.g. air, water, blood) and not discrete, countable entities (e.g. a tree, a leaf, a table top)

env_monitoring_zone.name: env_monitoring_zone
env_monitoring_zone.title: food production environmental monitoring zone
env_monitoring_zone.description: An environmental monitoring zone is a formal designation as part of an environmental monitoring program, in which areas of a food production facility are categorized, commonly as zones 1-4, based on likelihood or risk of foodborne pathogen contamination. This field accepts terms listed under food production environmental monitoring zone (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO). Please add a term to indicate the environmental monitoring zone the sample was taken from

escalator.name: escalator
escalator.title: escalator count
escalator.description: The number of escalators within the built structure

estimated_size.name: estimated_size
estimated_size.title: estimated size
estimated_size.description: The estimated size of the genome prior to sequencing. Of particular importance in the sequencing of (eukaryotic) genome which could remain in draft form for a long or unspecified period

ethnicity.name: ethnicity
ethnicity.title: ethnicity
ethnicity.description: A category of people who identify with each other, usually on the basis of presumed similarities such as a common language, ancestry, history, society, culture, nation or social treatment within their residing area. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_contemporary_ethnic_groups

ethylbenzene.name: ethylbenzene
ethylbenzene.title: ethylbenzene
ethylbenzene.description: Concentration of ethylbenzene in the sample

exp_duct.name: exp_duct
exp_duct.title: exposed ductwork
exp_duct.description: The amount of exposed ductwork in the room

exp_pipe.name: exp_pipe
exp_pipe.title: exposed pipes
exp_pipe.description: The number of exposed pipes in the room

experimental_factor.name: experimental_factor
experimental_factor.title: experimental factor
experimental_factor.description: Variable aspects of an experiment design that can be used to describe an experiment, or set of experiments, in an increasingly detailed manner. This field accepts ontology terms from Experimental Factor Ontology (EFO) and/or Ontology for Biomedical Investigations (OBI)

ext_door.name: ext_door
ext_door.title: exterior door count
ext_door.description: The number of exterior doors in the built structure

ext_wall_orient.name: ext_wall_orient
ext_wall_orient.title: orientations of exterior wall
ext_wall_orient.description: The orientation of the exterior wall

ext_window_orient.name: ext_window_orient
ext_window_orient.title: orientations of exterior window
ext_window_orient.description: The compass direction the exterior window of the room is facing

extr_weather_event.name: extr_weather_event
extr_weather_event.title: extreme weather event
extr_weather_event.description: Unusual weather events that may have affected microbial populations. Multiple terms can be separated by pipes, listed in reverse chronological order

extrachrom_elements.name: extrachrom_elements
extrachrom_elements.title: extrachromosomal elements
extrachrom_elements.description: Do plasmids exist of significant phenotypic consequence (e.g. ones that determine virulence or antibiotic resistance). Megaplasmids? Other plasmids (borrelia has 15+ plasmids)

extreme_event.name: extreme_event
extreme_event.title: history/extreme events
extreme_event.description: Unusual physical events that may have affected microbial populations

facility_type.name: facility_type
facility_type.title: facility type
facility_type.description: Establishment details about the type of facility where the sample was taken. This is independent of the specific product(s) within the facility

fao_class.name: fao_class
fao_class.title: soil_taxonomic/FAO classification
fao_class.description: Soil classification from the FAO World soil distribution from International Soil Reference and Information Centre (ISRIC). The list of available soil classifications can be found at https://www.isric.org/explore/world-soil-distribution

farm_equip.name: farm_equip
farm_equip.title: farm equipment used
farm_equip.description: List of equipment used for planting, fertilization, harvesting, irrigation, land levelling, residue management, weeding or transplanting during the growing season. This field accepts terms listed under agricultural implement (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/AGRO_00000416). Multiple terms can be separated by pipes

farm_equip_san.name: farm_equip_san
farm_equip_san.title: farm equipment sanitization
farm_equip_san.description: Method used to sanitize growing and harvesting equipment. This can including type and concentration of sanitizing solution. Multiple terms can be separated by one or more pipes
examples: hot water pressure wash, hypochlorite solution, 50 parts per million

farm_equip_san_freq.name: farm_equip_san_freq
farm_equip_san_freq.title: farm equipment sanitization frequency
farm_equip_san_freq.description: The number of times farm equipment is cleaned. Frequency of cleaning might be on a Daily basis, Weekly, Monthly, Quarterly or Annually

farm_equip_shared.name: farm_equip_shared
farm_equip_shared.title: equipment shared with other farms
farm_equip_shared.description: List of planting, growing or harvesting equipment shared with other farms. This field accepts terms listed under agricultural implement (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/AGRO_00000416). Multiple terms can be separated by pipes

farm_water_source.name: farm_water_source
farm_water_source.title: farm watering water source
farm_water_source.description: Source of water used on the farm for irrigation of crops or watering of livestock

feat_pred.name: feat_pred
feat_pred.title: feature prediction
feat_pred.description: Method used to predict UViGs features such as ORFs, integration site, etc

ferm_chem_add.name: ferm_chem_add
ferm_chem_add.title: fermentation chemical additives
ferm_chem_add.description: Any chemicals that are added to the fermentation process to achieve the desired final product

ferm_chem_add_perc.name: ferm_chem_add_perc
ferm_chem_add_perc.title: fermentation chemical additives percentage
ferm_chem_add_perc.description: The amount of chemical added to the fermentation process

ferm_headspace_oxy.name: ferm_headspace_oxy
ferm_headspace_oxy.title: fermentation headspace oxygen
ferm_headspace_oxy.description: The amount of headspace oxygen in a fermentation vessel

ferm_medium.name: ferm_medium
ferm_medium.title: fermentation medium
ferm_medium.description: The growth medium used for the fermented food fermentation process, which supplies the required nutrients. Usually this includes a carbon and nitrogen source, water, micronutrients and chemical additives

ferm_pH.name: ferm_pH
ferm_pH.title: fermentation pH
ferm_pH.description: The pH of the fermented food fermentation process

ferm_rel_humidity.name: ferm_rel_humidity
ferm_rel_humidity.title: fermentation relative humidity
ferm_rel_humidity.description: The relative humidity of the fermented food fermentation process

ferm_temp.name: ferm_temp
ferm_temp.title: fermentation temperature
ferm_temp.description: The temperature of the fermented food fermentation process

ferm_time.name: ferm_time
ferm_time.title: fermentation time
ferm_time.description: The time duration of the fermented food fermentation process

ferm_vessel.name: ferm_vessel
ferm_vessel.title: fermentation vessel
ferm_vessel.description: The type of vessel used for containment of the fermentation

fertilizer_admin.name: fertilizer_admin
fertilizer_admin.title: fertilizer administration
fertilizer_admin.description: Type of fertilizer or amendment added to the soil or water for the purpose of improving substrate health and quality for plant growth. This field accepts terms listed under agronomic fertilizer (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/AGRO_00002062). Multiple terms may apply and can be separated by pipes, listing in reverse chronological order

fertilizer_date.name: fertilizer_date
fertilizer_date.title: fertilizer administration date
fertilizer_date.description: Date of administration of soil amendment or fertilizer. Multiple terms may apply and can be separated by pipes, listing in reverse chronological order

fertilizer_regm.name: fertilizer_regm
fertilizer_regm.title: fertilizer regimen
fertilizer_regm.description: Information about treatment involving the use of fertilizers; should include the name of fertilizer, amount administered, treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment; can include multiple fertilizer regimens

field.name: field
field.title: field name
field.description: Name of the hydrocarbon field (e.g. Albacora)

filter_type.name: filter_type
filter_type.title: filter type
filter_type.description: A device which removes solid particulates or airborne molecular contaminants

fire.name: fire
fire.title: history/fire
fire.description: Historical and/or physical evidence of fire

flooding.name: flooding
flooding.title: history/flooding
flooding.description: Historical and/or physical evidence of flooding

floor_age.name: floor_age
floor_age.title: floor age
floor_age.description: The time period since installment of the carpet or flooring

floor_area.name: floor_area
floor_area.title: floor area
floor_area.description: The area of the floor space within the room

floor_cond.name: floor_cond
floor_cond.title: floor condition
floor_cond.description: The physical condition of the floor at the time of sampling; photos or video preferred; use drawings to indicate location of damaged areas

floor_count.name: floor_count
floor_count.title: floor count
floor_count.description: The number of floors in the building, including basements and mechanical penthouse

floor_finish_mat.name: floor_finish_mat
floor_finish_mat.title: floor finish material
floor_finish_mat.description: The floor covering type; the finished surface that is walked on

floor_struc.name: floor_struc
floor_struc.title: floor structure
floor_struc.description: Refers to the structural elements and subfloor upon which the finish flooring is installed

floor_thermal_mass.name: floor_thermal_mass
floor_thermal_mass.title: floor thermal mass
floor_thermal_mass.description: The ability of the floor to provide inertia against temperature fluctuations

floor_water_mold.name: floor_water_mold
floor_water_mold.title: floor signs of water/mold
floor_water_mold.description: Signs of the presence of mold or mildew in a room

fluor.name: fluor
fluor.title: fluorescence
fluor.description: Raw or converted fluorescence of water

foetal_health_stat.name: foetal_health_stat
foetal_health_stat.title: amniotic fluid/foetal health status
foetal_health_stat.description: Specification of foetal health status, should also include abortion

food_additive.name: food_additive
food_additive.title: food additive
food_additive.description: A substance or substances added to food to maintain or improve safety and freshness, to improve or maintain nutritional value, or improve taste, texture and appearance. This field accepts terms listed under food additive (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03412972). Multiple terms can be separated by one or more pipes, but please consider limiting this list to the top 5 ingredients listed in order as on the food label. See also, https://www.fda.gov/food/food-ingredients-packaging/overview-food-ingredients-additives-colors

food_allergen_label.name: food_allergen_label
food_allergen_label.title: food allergen labeling
food_allergen_label.description: A label indication that the product contains a recognized allergen. This field accepts terms listed under dietary claim or use (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03510213)
examples: food allergen labelling about crustaceans and products thereof [FOODON:03510215]

food_clean_proc.name: food_clean_proc
food_clean_proc.title: food cleaning process
food_clean_proc.description: The process of cleaning food to separate other environmental materials from the food source. Multiple terms can be separated by pipes

food_contact_surf.name: food_contact_surf
food_contact_surf.title: food contact surface
food_contact_surf.description: The specific container or coating materials in direct contact with the food. Multiple values can be assigned. This field accepts terms listed under food contact surface (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03500010)

food_contain_wrap.name: food_contain_wrap
food_contain_wrap.title: food container or wrapping
food_contain_wrap.description: Type of container or wrapping defined by the main container material, the container form, and the material of the liner lids or ends. Also type of container or wrapping by form; prefer description by material first, then by form. This field accepts terms listed under food container or wrapping (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03490100)

food_cooking_proc.name: food_cooking_proc
food_cooking_proc.title: food cooking process
food_cooking_proc.description: The transformation of raw food by the application of heat. This field accepts terms listed under food cooking (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03450002)

food_dis_point.name: food_dis_point
food_dis_point.title: food distribution point geographic location
food_dis_point.description: A reference to a place on the Earth, by its name or by its geographical location that refers to a distribution point along the food chain. This field accepts terms listed under geographic location (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GAZ_00000448). Reference: Adam Diamond, James Barham. Moving Food Along the Value Chain: Innovations in Regional Food Distribution. U.S. Dept. of Agriculture, Agricultural Marketing Service. Washington, DC. March 2012. http://dx.doi.org/10.9752/MS045.03-2012

food_dis_point_city.name: food_dis_point_city
food_dis_point_city.title: food distribution point geographic location (city)
food_dis_point_city.description: A reference to a place on the Earth, by its name or by its geographical location that refers to a distribution point along the food chain. This field accepts terms listed under geographic location (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GAZ_00000448). Reference: Adam Diamond, James Barham. Moving Food Along the Value Chain: Innovations in Regional Food Distribution. U.S. Dept. of Agriculture, Agricultural Marketing Service. Washington, DC. March 2012. http://dx.doi.org/10.9752/MS045.03-2012

food_harvest_proc.name: food_harvest_proc
food_harvest_proc.title: Food harvesting process
food_harvest_proc.description: A harvesting process is a process which takes in some food material from an individual or community of plant or animal organisms in a given context and time, and outputs a precursor or consumable food product. This may include a part of an organism or the whole, and may involve killing the organism

food_ingredient.name: food_ingredient
food_ingredient.title: food ingredient
food_ingredient.description: In this field, please list individual ingredients for multi-component food [FOODON:00002501] and simple foods that is not captured in food_type. Please use terms that are present in FoodOn. Multiple terms can be separated by one or more pipes |, but please consider limiting this list to the top 5 ingredients listed in order as on the food label. See also, https://www.fda.gov/food/food-ingredients-packaging/overview-food-ingredients-additives-colors

food_name_status.name: food_name_status
food_name_status.title: food product name legal status
food_name_status.description: A datum indicating that use of a food product name is regulated in some legal jurisdiction. This field accepts terms listed under food product name legal status (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03530087)

food_origin.name: food_origin
food_origin.title: food product origin geographic location
food_origin.description: A reference to a place on the Earth, by its name or by its geographical location that describes the origin of the food commodity, either in terms of its cultivation or production. This field accepts terms listed under geographic location (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GAZ_00000448)

food_pack_capacity.name: food_pack_capacity
food_pack_capacity.title: food package capacity
food_pack_capacity.description: The maximum number of product units within a package

food_pack_integrity.name: food_pack_integrity
food_pack_integrity.title: food packing medium integrity
food_pack_integrity.description: A term label and term id to describe the state of the packing material and text to explain the exact condition. This field accepts terms listed under food packing medium integrity (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03530218)

food_pack_medium.name: food_pack_medium
food_pack_medium.title: food packing medium
food_pack_medium.description: The medium in which the food is packed for preservation and handling or the medium surrounding homemade foods, e.g., peaches cooked in sugar syrup. The packing medium may provide a controlled environment for the food. It may also serve to improve palatability and consumer appeal. This includes edible packing media (e.g. fruit juice), gas other than air (e.g. carbon dioxide), vacuum packed, or packed with aerosol propellant. This field accepts terms under food packing medium (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03480020). Multiple terms may apply and can be separated by pipes

food_preserv_proc.name: food_preserv_proc
food_preserv_proc.title: food preservation process
food_preserv_proc.description: The methods contributing to the prevention or retardation of microbial, enzymatic or oxidative spoilage and thus to the extension of shelf life. This field accepts terms listed under food preservation process (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03470107)

food_prior_contact.name: food_prior_contact
food_prior_contact.title: material of contact prior to food packaging
food_prior_contact.description: The material the food contacted (e.g., was processed in) prior to packaging. This field accepts terms listed under material of contact prior to food packaging (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03530077). If the proper descriptor is not listed please use text to describe the material of contact prior to food packaging

food_prod.name: food_prod
food_prod.title: food production system characteristics
food_prod.description: Descriptors of the food production system or of the agricultural environment and growing conditions related to the farm production system, such as wild caught, organic, free-range, industrial, dairy, beef, domestic or cultivated food production. This field accepts terms listed under food production (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03530206). Multiple terms may apply and can be separated by pipes

food_prod_char.name: food_prod_char
food_prod_char.title: food production characteristics
food_prod_char.description: Descriptors of the food production system such as wild caught, free-range, organic, free-range, industrial, dairy, beef

food_prod_synonym.name: food_prod_synonym
food_prod_synonym.title: food product synonym
food_prod_synonym.description: Other names by which the food product is known by (e.g., regional or non-English names)

food_product_qual.name: food_product_qual
food_product_qual.title: food product by quality
food_product_qual.description: Descriptors for describing food visually or via other senses, which is useful for tasks like food inspection where little prior knowledge of how the food came to be is available. Some terms like "food (frozen)" are both a quality descriptor and the output of a process. This field accepts terms listed under food product by quality (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_00002454)

food_product_type.name: food_product_type
food_product_type.title: food product type
food_product_type.description: A food product type is a class of food products that is differentiated by its food composition (e.g., single- or multi-ingredient), processing and/or consumption characteristics. This does not include brand name products but it may include generic food dish categories. This field accepts terms under food product type (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON:03400361). For terms related to food product for an animal, consult food product for animal (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03309997). If the proper descriptor is not listed please use text to describe the food type. Multiple terms can be separated by one or more pipes

food_quality_date.name: food_quality_date
food_quality_date.title: food quality date
food_quality_date.description: The date recommended for the use of the product while at peak quality, this date is not a reflection of safety unless used on infant formula this date is not a reflection of safety and is typically labeled on a food product as "best if used by," best by," "use by," or "freeze by."

food_source.name: food_source
food_source.title: food source
food_source.description: Type of plant or animal from which the food product or its major ingredient is derived or a chemical food source [FDA CFSAN 1995]

food_source_age.name: food_source_age
food_source_age.title: food source age
food_source_age.description: The age of the food source host organism. Depending on the type of host organism, age may be more appropriate to report in days, weeks, or years

food_trace_list.name: food_trace_list
food_trace_list.title: food traceability list category
food_trace_list.description: The FDA is proposing to establish additional traceability recordkeeping requirements (beyond what is already required in existing regulations) for persons who manufacture, process, pack, or hold foods the Agency has designated for inclusion on the Food Traceability List. The Food Traceability List (FTL) identifies the foods for which the additional traceability records described in the proposed rule would be required. The term Food Traceability List (FTL) refers not only to the foods specifically listed (https://www.fda.gov/media/142303/download), but also to any foods that contain listed foods as ingredients

food_trav_mode.name: food_trav_mode
food_trav_mode.title: food shipping transportation method
food_trav_mode.description: A descriptor for the method of movement of food commodity along the food distribution system. This field accepts terms listed under travel mode (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GENEPIO_0001064). If the proper descrptor is not listed please use text to describe the mode of travel. Multiple terms can be separated by one or more pipes

food_trav_vehic.name: food_trav_vehic
food_trav_vehic.title: food shipping transportation vehicle
food_trav_vehic.description: A descriptor for the mobile machine which is used to transport food commodities along the food distribution system. This field accepts terms listed under vehicle (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_01000604). If the proper descrptor is not listed please use text to describe the mode of travel. Multiple terms can be separated by one or more pipes

food_treat_proc.name: food_treat_proc
food_treat_proc.title: food treatment process
food_treat_proc.description: Used to specifically characterize a food product based on the treatment or processes applied to the product or any indexed ingredient. The processes include adding, substituting or removing components or modifying the food or component, e.g., through fermentation. Multiple values can be assigned. This fields accepts terms listed under food treatment process (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03460111)

freq_clean.name: freq_clean
freq_clean.title: frequency of cleaning
freq_clean.description: The number of times the sample location is cleaned. Frequency of cleaning might be on a Daily basis, Weekly, Monthly, Quarterly or Annually

freq_cook.name: freq_cook
freq_cook.title: frequency of cooking
freq_cook.description: The number of times a meal is cooked per week

fungicide_regm.name: fungicide_regm
fungicide_regm.title: fungicide regimen
fungicide_regm.description: Information about treatment involving use of fungicides; should include the name of fungicide, amount administered, treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment; can include multiple fungicide regimens

furniture.name: furniture
furniture.title: furniture
furniture.description: The types of furniture present in the sampled room

gaseous_environment.name: gaseous_environment
gaseous_environment.title: gaseous environment
gaseous_environment.description: Use of conditions with differing gaseous environments; should include the name of gaseous compound, amount administered, treatment duration, interval and total experimental duration; can include multiple gaseous environment regimens

gaseous_substances.name: gaseous_substances
gaseous_substances.title: gaseous substances
gaseous_substances.description: Amount or concentration of substances such as hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide, methane, etc.; can include multiple substances

gastrointest_disord.name: gastrointest_disord
gastrointest_disord.title: gastrointestinal tract disorder
gastrointest_disord.description: History of gastrointestinal tract disorders; can include multiple disorders. History of blood disorders; can include multiple disorders. The terms should be chosen from the DO (Human Disease Ontology) at http://www.disease-ontology.org, gastrointestinal system disease (https://disease-ontology.org/?id=DOID:77)

gender_restroom.name: gender_restroom
gender_restroom.title: gender of restroom
gender_restroom.description: The gender type of the restroom

genetic_mod.name: genetic_mod
genetic_mod.title: genetic modification
genetic_mod.description: Genetic modifications of the genome of an organism, which may occur naturally by spontaneous mutation, or be introduced by some experimental means, e.g. specification of a transgene or the gene knocked-out or details of transient transfection

geo_loc_name.name: geo_loc_name
geo_loc_name.title: geographic location (country and/or sea,region)
geo_loc_name.description: The geographical origin of the sample as defined by the country or sea name followed by specific region name. Country or sea names should be chosen from the INSDC country list (http://insdc.org/country.html), or the GAZ ontology (http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/GAZ)

gravidity.name: gravidity
gravidity.title: gravidity
gravidity.description: Whether or not subject is gravid, and if yes date due or date post-conception, specifying which is used

gravity.name: gravity
gravity.title: gravity
gravity.description: Information about treatment involving use of gravity factor to study various types of responses in presence, absence or modified levels of gravity; treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment; can include multiple treatments

growth_facil.name: growth_facil
growth_facil.title: growth facility
growth_facil.description: Type of facility where the sampled plant was grown; controlled vocabulary: growth chamber, open top chamber, glasshouse, experimental garden, field. Alternatively use Crop Ontology (CO) terms, see http://www.cropontology.org/ontology/CO_715/Crop%20Research

growth_habit.name: growth_habit
growth_habit.title: growth habit
growth_habit.description: Characteristic shape, appearance or growth form of a plant species

growth_hormone_regm.name: growth_hormone_regm
growth_hormone_regm.title: growth hormone regimen
growth_hormone_regm.description: Information about treatment involving use of growth hormones; should include the name of growth hormone, amount administered, treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment; can include multiple growth hormone regimens

growth_medium.name: growth_medium
growth_medium.title: growth medium
growth_medium.description: A liquid or gel containing nutrients, salts, and other factors formulated to support the growth of microorganisms, cells, or plants (National Cancer Institute Thesaurus). The name of the medium used to grow the microorganism

gynecologic_disord.name: gynecologic_disord
gynecologic_disord.title: gynecological disorder
gynecologic_disord.description: History of gynecological disorders; can include multiple disorders. The terms should be chosen from the DO (Human Disease Ontology) at http://www.disease-ontology.org, female reproductive system disease (https://disease-ontology.org/?id=DOID:229)

hall_count.name: hall_count
hall_count.title: hallway/corridor count
hall_count.description: The total count of hallways and corridors in the built structure

handidness.name: handidness
handidness.title: handidness
handidness.description: The handidness of the individual sampled

hc_produced.name: hc_produced
hc_produced.title: hydrocarbon type produced
hc_produced.description: Main hydrocarbon type produced from resource (i.e. Oil, gas, condensate, etc). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

hcr.name: hcr
hcr.title: hydrocarbon resource type
hcr.description: Main Hydrocarbon Resource type. The term "Hydrocarbon Resource" HCR defined as a natural environmental feature containing large amounts of hydrocarbons at high concentrations potentially suitable for commercial exploitation. This term should not be confused with the Hydrocarbon Occurrence term which also includes hydrocarbon-rich environments with currently limited commercial interest such as seeps, outcrops, gas hydrates etc. If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

hcr_fw_salinity.name: hcr_fw_salinity
hcr_fw_salinity.title: formation water salinity
hcr_fw_salinity.description: Original formation water salinity (prior to secondary recovery e.g. Waterflooding) expressed as TDS

hcr_geol_age.name: hcr_geol_age
hcr_geol_age.title: hydrocarbon resource geological age
hcr_geol_age.description: Geological age of hydrocarbon resource (Additional info: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Period_(geology)). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

hcr_pressure.name: hcr_pressure
hcr_pressure.title: hydrocarbon resource original pressure
hcr_pressure.description: Original pressure of the hydrocarbon resource

hcr_temp.name: hcr_temp
hcr_temp.title: hydrocarbon resource original temperature
hcr_temp.description: Original temperature of the hydrocarbon resource

heat_cool_type.name: heat_cool_type
heat_cool_type.title: heating and cooling system type
heat_cool_type.description: Methods of conditioning or heating a room or building

heat_deliv_loc.name: heat_deliv_loc
heat_deliv_loc.title: heating delivery locations
heat_deliv_loc.description: The location of heat delivery within the room

heat_sys_deliv_meth.name: heat_sys_deliv_meth
heat_sys_deliv_meth.title: heating system delivery method
heat_sys_deliv_meth.description: The method by which the heat is delivered through the system

heavy_metals.name: heavy_metals
heavy_metals.title: extreme_unusual_properties/heavy metals
heavy_metals.description: Heavy metals present in the sequenced sample and their concentrations. For multiple heavy metals and concentrations, add multiple copies of this field

heavy_metals_meth.name: heavy_metals_meth
heavy_metals_meth.title: extreme_unusual_properties/heavy metals method
heavy_metals_meth.description: Reference or method used in determining heavy metals

height_carper_fiber.name: height_carper_fiber
height_carper_fiber.title: height carpet fiber mat
height_carper_fiber.description: The average carpet fiber height in the indoor environment

herbicide_regm.name: herbicide_regm
herbicide_regm.title: herbicide regimen
herbicide_regm.description: Information about treatment involving use of herbicides; information about treatment involving use of growth hormones; should include the name of herbicide, amount administered, treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment; can include multiple regimens

horizon_meth.name: horizon_meth
horizon_meth.title: horizon method
horizon_meth.description: Reference or method used in determining the horizon

host_age.name: host_age
host_age.title: host age
host_age.description: Age of host at the time of sampling; relevant scale depends on species and study, e.g. Could be seconds for amoebae or centuries for trees

host_body_habitat.name: host_body_habitat
host_body_habitat.title: host body habitat
host_body_habitat.description: Original body habitat where the sample was obtained from

host_body_mass_index.name: host_body_mass_index
host_body_mass_index.title: host body-mass index
host_body_mass_index.description: Body mass index, calculated as weight/(height)squared

host_body_product.name: host_body_product
host_body_product.title: host body product
host_body_product.description: Substance produced by the body, e.g. Stool, mucus, where the sample was obtained from. Use terms from the foundational model of anatomy ontology (fma) or Uber-anatomy ontology (UBERON)

host_body_site.name: host_body_site
host_body_site.title: host body site
host_body_site.description: Name of body site where the sample was obtained from, such as a specific organ or tissue (tongue, lung etc...). Use terms from the foundational model of anatomy ontology (fma) or the Uber-anatomy ontology (UBERON)

host_body_temp.name: host_body_temp
host_body_temp.title: host body temperature
host_body_temp.description: Core body temperature of the host when sample was collected

host_cellular_loc.name: host_cellular_loc
host_cellular_loc.title: host cellular location
host_cellular_loc.description: The localization of the symbiotic host organism within the host from which it was sampled: e.g. intracellular if the symbiotic host organism is localized within the cells or extracellular if the symbiotic host organism is localized outside of cells

host_dependence.name: host_dependence
host_dependence.title: host dependence
host_dependence.description: Type of host dependence for the symbiotic host organism to its host

host_diet.name: host_diet
host_diet.title: host diet
host_diet.description: Type of diet depending on the host, for animals omnivore, herbivore etc., for humans high-fat, meditteranean etc.; can include multiple diet types

host_disease_stat.name: host_disease_stat
host_disease_stat.title: host disease status
host_disease_stat.description: List of diseases with which the host has been diagnosed; can include multiple diagnoses. The value of the field depends on host; for humans the terms should be chosen from the DO (Human Disease Ontology) at https://www.disease-ontology.org, non-human host diseases are free text

host_fam_rel.name: host_fam_rel
host_fam_rel.title: host family relationship
host_fam_rel.description: Relationships to other hosts in the same study; can include multiple relationships

host_growth_cond.name: host_growth_cond
host_growth_cond.title: host growth conditions
host_growth_cond.description: Literature reference giving growth conditions of the host

host_hiv_stat.name: host_hiv_stat
host_hiv_stat.title: host HIV status
host_hiv_stat.description: HIV status of subject, if yes HAART initiation status should also be indicated as [YES or NO]

host_infra_spec_name.name: host_infra_spec_name
host_infra_spec_name.title: host infra-specific name
host_infra_spec_name.description: Taxonomic information about the host below subspecies level

host_infra_spec_rank.name: host_infra_spec_rank
host_infra_spec_rank.title: host infra-specific rank
host_infra_spec_rank.description: Taxonomic rank information about the host below subspecies level, such as variety, form, rank etc

host_last_meal.name: host_last_meal
host_last_meal.title: host last meal
host_last_meal.description: Content of last meal and time since feeding; can include multiple values

host_life_stage.name: host_life_stage
host_life_stage.title: host life stage
host_life_stage.description: Description of life stage of host

host_number.name: host_number
host_number.title: host number individual
host_number.description: Number of symbiotic host individuals pooled at the time of collection

host_occupation.name: host_occupation
host_occupation.title: host occupation
host_occupation.description: Most frequent job performed by subject
comments: Couldn't convert host_occupation with value veterinary to integer
almost all host_occupation values in the NCBI biosample_set are strings, not integers

host_of_host_coinf.name: host_of_host_coinf
host_of_host_coinf.title: observed coinfecting organisms in host of host
host_of_host_coinf.description: The taxonomic name of any coinfecting organism observed in a symbiotic relationship with the host of the sampled host organism. e.g. where a sample collected from a host trematode species (A) which was collected from a host_of_host fish (B) that was also infected with a nematode (C), the value here would be (C) the nematode {species name} or {common name}. Multiple co-infecting species may be added in a comma-separated list. For listing symbiotic organisms associated with the host (A) use the term Observed host symbiont

host_of_host_disease.name: host_of_host_disease
host_of_host_disease.title: host of the symbiotic host disease status
host_of_host_disease.description: List of diseases with which the host of the symbiotic host organism has been diagnosed; can include multiple diagnoses. The value of the field depends on host; for humans the terms should be chosen from the DO (Human Disease Ontology) at https://www.disease-ontology.org, non-human host diseases are free text

host_of_host_env_loc.name: host_of_host_env_loc
host_of_host_env_loc.title: host of the symbiotic host local environmental context
host_of_host_env_loc.description: For a symbiotic host organism the local anatomical environment within its host may have causal influences. Report the anatomical entity(s) which are in the direct environment of the symbiotic host organism being sampled and which you believe have significant causal influences on your sample or specimen. For example, if the symbiotic host organism being sampled is an intestinal worm, its local environmental context will be the term for intestine from UBERON (http://uberon.github.io/)

host_of_host_env_med.name: host_of_host_env_med
host_of_host_env_med.title: host of the symbiotic host environmental medium
host_of_host_env_med.description: Report the environmental material(s) immediately surrounding the symbiotic host organism at the time of sampling. This usually will be a tissue or substance type from the host, but may be another material if the symbiont is external to the host.
