We recommend using classes from the UBERON ontology, but subclasses of 'environmental material' (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00010483) may also be used. EnvO documentation about how to use the field: https://github.com/EnvironmentOntology/envo/wiki/Using-ENVO-with-MIxS . Terms from other OBO ontologies are permissible as long as they reference mass/volume nouns (e.g. air, water, blood) and not discrete, countable entities (e.g. intestines, heart).MIxS . Terms from other OBO ontologies are permissible as long as they reference mass/volume nouns (e.g. air, water, blood) and not discrete, countable entities (e.g. intestines, heart)

host_of_host_fam_rel.name: host_of_host_fam_rel
host_of_host_fam_rel.title: host of the symbiotic host family relationship
host_of_host_fam_rel.description: Familial relationship of the host of the symbiotic host organisms to other hosts of symbiotic host organism in the same study; can include multiple relationships

host_of_host_geno.name: host_of_host_geno
host_of_host_geno.title: host of the symbiotic host genotype
host_of_host_geno.description: Observed genotype of the host of the symbiotic host organism

host_of_host_gravid.name: host_of_host_gravid
host_of_host_gravid.title: host of the symbiotic host gravidity
host_of_host_gravid.description: Whether or not the host of the symbiotic host organism is gravid, and if yes date due or date post-conception, specifying which is used

host_of_host_infname.name: host_of_host_infname
host_of_host_infname.title: host of the symbiotic host infra-specific name
host_of_host_infname.description: Taxonomic name information of the host of the symbiotic host organism below subspecies level

host_of_host_infrank.name: host_of_host_infrank
host_of_host_infrank.title: host of the symbiotic host infra-specific rank
host_of_host_infrank.description: Taxonomic rank information about the host of the symbiotic host organism below subspecies level, such as variety, form, rank etc

host_of_host_name.name: host_of_host_name
host_of_host_name.title: host of the symbiotic host common name
host_of_host_name.description: Common name of the host of the symbiotic host organism

host_of_host_pheno.name: host_of_host_pheno
host_of_host_pheno.title: host of the symbiotic host phenotype
host_of_host_pheno.description: Phenotype of the host of the symbiotic host organism. For phenotypic quality ontology (PATO) terms, see http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/pato

host_of_host_sub_id.name: host_of_host_sub_id
host_of_host_sub_id.title: host of the symbiotic host subject id
host_of_host_sub_id.description: A unique identifier by which each host of the symbiotic host organism subject can be referred to, de-identified, e.g. #H14

host_of_host_taxid.name: host_of_host_taxid
host_of_host_taxid.title: host of the symbiotic host taxon id
host_of_host_taxid.description: NCBI taxon id of the host of the symbiotic host organism

host_of_host_totmass.name: host_of_host_totmass
host_of_host_totmass.title: host of the symbiotic host total mass
host_of_host_totmass.description: Total mass of the host of the symbiotic host organism at collection, the unit depends on the host

host_phenotype.name: host_phenotype
host_phenotype.title: host phenotype
host_phenotype.description: Phenotype of human or other host. Use terms from the phenotypic quality ontology (pato) or the Human Phenotype Ontology (HP)

host_pred_appr.name: host_pred_appr
host_pred_appr.title: host prediction approach
host_pred_appr.description: Tool or approach used for host prediction

host_pred_est_acc.name: host_pred_est_acc
host_pred_est_acc.title: host prediction estimated accuracy
host_pred_est_acc.description: For each tool or approach used for host prediction, estimated false discovery rates should be included, either computed de novo or from the literature
examples: CRISPR spacer match: 0 or 1 mismatches, estimated 8% FDR at the host genus rank (Edwards et al. 2016 doi:10.1093/femsre/fuv048)

host_pulse.name: host_pulse
host_pulse.title: host pulse
host_pulse.description: Resting pulse, measured as beats per minute

host_sex.name: host_sex
host_sex.title: host sex
host_sex.description: Gender or physical sex of the host
comments: example of non-binary from Excel sheets does not match any of the enumerated values

host_spec_range.name: host_spec_range
host_spec_range.title: host specificity or range
host_spec_range.description: The range and diversity of host species that an organism is capable of infecting, defined by NCBI taxonomy identifier

host_specificity.name: host_specificity
host_specificity.title: host specificity
host_specificity.description: Level of specificity of symbiont-host interaction: e.g. generalist (symbiont able to establish associations with distantly related hosts) or species-specific

host_subject_id.name: host_subject_id
host_subject_id.title: host subject id
host_subject_id.description: A unique identifier by which each subject can be referred to, de-identified

host_subspecf_genlin.name: host_subspecf_genlin
host_subspecf_genlin.title: host subspecific genetic lineage
host_subspecf_genlin.description: Information about the genetic distinctness of the host organism below the subspecies level e.g., serovar, serotype, biotype, ecotype, variety, cultivar, or any relevant genetic typing schemes like Group I plasmid. Subspecies should not be recorded in this term, but in the NCBI taxonomy. Supply both the lineage name and the lineage rank separated by a colon, e.g., biovar:abc123

host_substrate.name: host_substrate
host_substrate.title: host substrate
host_substrate.description: The growth substrate of the host

host_symbiont.name: host_symbiont
host_symbiont.title: observed host symbionts
host_symbiont.description: The taxonomic name of the organism(s) found living in mutualistic, commensalistic, or parasitic symbiosis with the specific host. The sampled symbiont can have its own symbionts. For example, parasites may have hyperparasites (=parasites of the parasite)

host_taxid.name: host_taxid
host_taxid.title: host taxid
host_taxid.description: NCBI taxon id of the host, e.g. 9606

host_tot_mass.name: host_tot_mass
host_tot_mass.title: host total mass
host_tot_mass.description: Total mass of the host at collection, the unit depends on host

hrt.name: hrt
hrt.title: HRT
hrt.description: Whether subject had hormone replacement theraphy, and if yes start date

humidity.name: humidity
humidity.title: humidity
humidity.description: Amount of water vapour in the air, at the time of sampling

humidity_regm.name: humidity_regm
humidity_regm.title: humidity regimen
humidity_regm.description: Information about treatment involving an exposure to varying degree of humidity; information about treatment involving use of growth hormones; should include amount of humidity administered, treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment; can include multiple regimens

hygienic_area.name: hygienic_area
hygienic_area.title: hygienic food production area
hygienic_area.description: The subdivision of areas within a food production facility according to hygienic requirements. This field accepts terms listed under hygienic food production area (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO). Please add a term that most accurately indicates the hygienic area your sample was taken from according to the definitions provided

hysterectomy.name: hysterectomy
hysterectomy.title: hysterectomy
hysterectomy.description: Specification of whether hysterectomy was performed

indoor_space.name: indoor_space
indoor_space.title: indoor space
indoor_space.description: A distinguishable space within a structure, the purpose for which discrete areas of a building is used

indust_eff_percent.name: indust_eff_percent
indust_eff_percent.title: industrial effluent percent
indust_eff_percent.description: Percentage of industrial effluents received by wastewater treatment plant

inorg_particles.name: inorg_particles
inorg_particles.title: inorganic particles
inorg_particles.description: Concentration of particles such as sand, grit, metal particles, ceramics, etc.; can include multiple particles

inside_lux.name: inside_lux
inside_lux.title: inside lux light
inside_lux.description: The recorded value at sampling time (power density)

int_wall_cond.name: int_wall_cond
int_wall_cond.title: interior wall condition
int_wall_cond.description: The physical condition of the wall at the time of sampling; photos or video preferred; use drawings to indicate location of damaged areas

intended_consumer.name: intended_consumer
intended_consumer.title: intended consumer
intended_consumer.description: Food consumer type, human or animal, for which the food product is produced and marketed. This field accepts terms listed under food consumer group (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03510136) or NCBI taxid

isol_growth_condt.name: isol_growth_condt
isol_growth_condt.title: isolation and growth condition
isol_growth_condt.description: Publication reference in the form of pubmed ID (pmid), digital object identifier (doi) or url for isolation and growth condition specifications of the organism/material

iw_bt_date_well.name: iw_bt_date_well
iw_bt_date_well.title: injection water breakthrough date of specific well
iw_bt_date_well.description: Injection water breakthrough date per well following a secondary and/or tertiary recovery

iwf.name: iwf
iwf.title: injection water fraction
iwf.description: Proportion of the produced fluids derived from injected water at the time of sampling. (e.g. 87%)

kidney_disord.name: kidney_disord
kidney_disord.title: urine/kidney disorder
kidney_disord.description: History of kidney disorders; can include multiple disorders. The terms should be chosen from the DO (Human Disease Ontology) at http://www.disease-ontology.org, kidney disease (https://disease-ontology.org/?id=DOID:557)

last_clean.name: last_clean
last_clean.title: last time swept/mopped/vacuumed
last_clean.description: The last time the floor was cleaned (swept, mopped, vacuumed)

lat_lon.name: lat_lon
lat_lon.title: geographic location (latitude and longitude)
lat_lon.description: The geographical origin of the sample as defined by latitude and longitude. The values should be reported in decimal degrees, limited to 8 decimal points, and in WGS84 system

lib_layout.name: lib_layout
lib_layout.title: library layout
lib_layout.description: Specify whether to expect single, paired, or other configuration of reads

lib_reads_seqd.name: lib_reads_seqd
lib_reads_seqd.title: library reads sequenced
lib_reads_seqd.description: Total number of clones sequenced from the library

lib_screen.name: lib_screen
lib_screen.title: library screening strategy
lib_screen.description: Specific enrichment or screening methods applied before and/or after creating libraries

lib_size.name: lib_size
lib_size.title: library size
lib_size.description: Total number of clones in the library prepared for the project

lib_vector.name: lib_vector
lib_vector.title: library vector
lib_vector.description: Cloning vector type(s) used in construction of libraries

library_prep_kit.name: library_prep_kit
library_prep_kit.title: library preparation kit
library_prep_kit.description: Packaged kits (containing adapters, indexes, enzymes, buffers etc.), tailored for specific sequencing workflows, which allow the simplified preparation of sequencing-ready libraries for small genomes, amplicons, and plasmids

light_regm.name: light_regm
light_regm.title: light regimen
light_regm.description: Information about treatment(s) involving exposure to light, including both light intensity and quality

light_type.name: light_type
light_type.title: light type
light_type.description: Application of light to achieve some practical or aesthetic effect. Lighting includes the use of both artificial light sources such as lamps and light fixtures, as well as natural illumination by capturing daylight. Can also include absence of light

link_addit_analys.name: link_addit_analys
link_addit_analys.title: links to additional analysis
link_addit_analys.description: Link to additional analysis results performed on the sample

link_class_info.name: link_class_info
link_class_info.title: link to classification information
link_class_info.description: Link to digitized soil maps or other soil classification information

lithology.name: lithology
lithology.title: lithology
lithology.description: Hydrocarbon resource main lithology (Additional information: http://petrowiki.org/Lithology_and_rock_type_determination). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

liver_disord.name: liver_disord
liver_disord.title: liver disorder
liver_disord.description: History of liver disorders; can include multiple disorders. The terms should be chosen from the DO (Human Disease Ontology) at http://www.disease-ontology.org, liver disease (https://disease-ontology.org/?id=DOID:409)

local_class.name: local_class
local_class.title: soil_taxonomic/local classification
local_class.description: Soil classification based on local soil classification system

local_class_meth.name: local_class_meth
local_class_meth.title: soil_taxonomic/local classification method
local_class_meth.description: Reference or method used in determining the local soil classification

lot_number.name: lot_number
lot_number.title: lot number
lot_number.description: A distinctive alpha-numeric identification code assigned by the manufacturer or distributor to a specific quantity of manufactured material or product within a batch. Synonym: Batch Number. The submitter should provide lot number of the item followed by the item name for which the lot number was provided

mag_cov_software.name: mag_cov_software
mag_cov_software.title: MAG coverage software
mag_cov_software.description: Tool(s) used to determine the genome coverage if coverage is used as a binning parameter in the extraction of genomes from metagenomic datasets

magnesium.name: magnesium
magnesium.title: magnesium
magnesium.description: Concentration of magnesium in the sample

maternal_health_stat.name: maternal_health_stat
maternal_health_stat.title: amniotic fluid/maternal health status
maternal_health_stat.description: Specification of the maternal health status

max_occup.name: max_occup
max_occup.title: maximum occupancy
max_occup.description: The maximum amount of people allowed in the indoor environment

mean_peak_frict_vel.name: mean_peak_frict_vel
mean_peak_frict_vel.title: mean peak friction velocity
mean_peak_frict_vel.description: Measurement of mean peak friction velocity

mechanical_damage.name: mechanical_damage
mechanical_damage.title: mechanical damage
mechanical_damage.description: Information about any mechanical damage exerted on the plant; can include multiple damages and sites

medic_hist_perform.name: medic_hist_perform
medic_hist_perform.title: medical history performed
medic_hist_perform.description: Whether full medical history was collected

methane.name: methane
methane.title: methane
methane.description: Methane (gas) amount or concentration at the time of sampling

micro_biomass_meth.name: micro_biomass_meth
micro_biomass_meth.title: microbial biomass method
micro_biomass_meth.description: Reference or method used in determining microbial biomass

microb_cult_med.name: microb_cult_med
microb_cult_med.title: microbiological culture medium
microb_cult_med.description: A culture medium used to select for, grow, and maintain prokaryotic microorganisms. Can be in either liquid (broth) or solidified (e.g. with agar) forms. This field accepts terms listed under microbiological culture medium (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/MICRO_0000067). If the proper descriptor is not listed please use text to describe the culture medium

microb_start.name: microb_start
microb_start.title: microbial starter
microb_start.description: Any type of microorganisms used in food production. This field accepts terms listed under live organisms for food production (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_0344453)

microb_start_count.name: microb_start_count
microb_start_count.title: microbial starter organism count
microb_start_count.description: Total cell count of starter culture per gram, volume or area of sample and the method that was used for the enumeration (e.g. qPCR, atp, mpn, etc.) should also be provided. (example : total prokaryotes; 3.5e7 cells per ml; qPCR)
examples: total prokaryotes;3.5e9 colony forming units per milliliter;spread plate

microb_start_inoc.name: microb_start_inoc
microb_start_inoc.title: microbial starter inoculation
microb_start_inoc.description: The amount of starter culture used to inoculate a new batch

microb_start_prep.name: microb_start_prep
microb_start_prep.title: microbial starter preparation
microb_start_prep.description: Information about the protocol or method used to prepare the starter inoculum
examples: liquid starter culture, propagated 3 cycles in milk prior to inoculation

microb_start_source.name: microb_start_source
microb_start_source.title: microbial starter source
microb_start_source.description: The source from which the microbial starter culture was sourced. If commercially supplied, list supplier

microb_start_taxID.name: microb_start_taxID
microb_start_taxID.title: microbial starter NCBI taxonomy ID
microb_start_taxID.description: Please include Genus species and strain ID, if known of microorganisms used in food production. For complex communities, pipes can be used to separate two or more microbes

microbial_biomass.name: microbial_biomass
microbial_biomass.title: microbial biomass
microbial_biomass.description: The part of the organic matter in the soil that constitutes living microorganisms smaller than 5-10 micrometer. If you keep this, you would need to have correction factors used for conversion to the final units

mid.name: mid
mid.title: multiplex identifiers
mid.description: Molecular barcodes, called Multiplex Identifiers (MIDs), that are used to specifically tag unique samples in a sequencing run. Sequence should be reported in uppercase letters

mineral_nutr_regm.name: mineral_nutr_regm
mineral_nutr_regm.title: mineral nutrient regimen
mineral_nutr_regm.description: Information about treatment involving the use of mineral supplements; should include the name of mineral nutrient, amount administered, treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment; can include multiple mineral nutrient regimens

misc_param.name: misc_param
misc_param.title: miscellaneous parameter
misc_param.description: Any other measurement performed or parameter collected, that is not listed here
examples: Bicarbonate ion concentration;2075 micromole per kilogram

mode_transmission.name: mode_transmission
mode_transmission.title: mode of transmission
mode_transmission.description: The process through which the symbiotic host organism entered the host from which it was sampled

n_alkanes.name: n_alkanes
n_alkanes.title: n-alkanes
n_alkanes.description: Concentration of n-alkanes; can include multiple n-alkanes

neg_cont_type.name: neg_cont_type
neg_cont_type.title: negative control type
neg_cont_type.description: The substance or equipment used as a negative control in an investigation

nitrate.name: nitrate
nitrate.title: nitrate
nitrate.description: Concentration of nitrate in the sample

nitrite.name: nitrite
nitrite.title: nitrite
nitrite.description: Concentration of nitrite in the sample

non_min_nutr_regm.name: non_min_nutr_regm
non_min_nutr_regm.title: non-mineral nutrient regimen
non_min_nutr_regm.description: Information about treatment involving the exposure of plant to non-mineral nutrient such as oxygen, hydrogen or carbon; should include the name of non-mineral nutrient, amount administered, treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment; can include multiple non-mineral nutrient regimens

nose_mouth_teeth_throat_disord.name: nose_mouth_teeth_throat_disord
nose_mouth_teeth_throat_disord.title: nose/mouth/teeth/throat disorder
nose_mouth_teeth_throat_disord.description: History of nose/mouth/teeth/throat disorders; can include multiple disorders. The terms should be chosen from the DO (Human Disease Ontology) at http://www.disease-ontology.org, nose disease (https://disease-ontology.org/?id=DOID:2825), mouth disease (https://disease-ontology.org/?id=DOID:403), tooth disease (https://disease-ontology.org/?id=DOID:1091), or upper respiratory tract disease (https://disease-ontology.org/?id=DOID:974)

nose_throat_disord.name: nose_throat_disord
nose_throat_disord.title: nose throat disorder
nose_throat_disord.description: Report any history of nose, mouth, teeth and/or throat disorders in the subject. May include multiple disorders.
comments: The terms should be chosen from the DO (Human Disease Ontology) at http://www.disease-ontology.org, lung disease (https://disease-ontology.org/?id=DOID:850), upper respiratory tract disease (https://disease-ontology.org/?id=DOID:974)

nucl_acid_amp.name: nucl_acid_amp
nucl_acid_amp.title: nucleic acid amplification
nucl_acid_amp.description: A link to a literature reference, electronic resource or a standard operating procedure (SOP), that describes the enzymatic amplification (PCR, TMA, NASBA) of specific nucleic acids

nucl_acid_ext.name: nucl_acid_ext
nucl_acid_ext.title: nucleic acid extraction
nucl_acid_ext.description: A link to a literature reference, electronic resource or a standard operating procedure (SOP), that describes the material separation to recover the nucleic acid fraction from a sample

nucl_acid_ext_kit.name: nucl_acid_ext_kit
nucl_acid_ext_kit.title: nucleic acid extraction kit
nucl_acid_ext_kit.description: The name of the extraction kit used to recover the nucleic acid fraction of an input material is performed

num_replicons.name: num_replicons
num_replicons.title: number of replicons
num_replicons.description: Reports the number of replicons in a nuclear genome of eukaryotes, in the genome of a bacterium or archaea or the number of segments in a segmented virus. Always applied to the haploid chromosome count of a eukaryote

num_samp_collect.name: num_samp_collect
num_samp_collect.title: number of samples collected
num_samp_collect.description: The number of samples collected during the current sampling event

number_contig.name: number_contig
number_contig.title: number of contigs
number_contig.description: Total number of contigs in the cleaned/submitted assembly that makes up a given genome, SAG, MAG, or UViG

number_pets.name: number_pets
number_pets.title: number of pets
number_pets.description: The number of pets residing in the sampled space

number_plants.name: number_plants
number_plants.title: number of houseplants
number_plants.description: The number of plant(s) in the sampling space

number_resident.name: number_resident
number_resident.title: number of residents
number_resident.description: The number of individuals currently occupying in the sampling location

occup_density_samp.name: occup_density_samp
occup_density_samp.title: occupant density at sampling
occup_density_samp.description: Average number of occupants at time of sampling per square footage

occup_document.name: occup_document
occup_document.title: occupancy documentation
occup_document.description: The type of documentation of occupancy

occup_samp.name: occup_samp
occup_samp.title: occupancy at sampling
occup_samp.description: Number of occupants present at time of sample within the given space

org_count_qpcr_info.name: org_count_qpcr_info
org_count_qpcr_info.title: organism count qPCR information
org_count_qpcr_info.description: If qpcr was used for the cell count, the target gene name, the primer sequence and the cycling conditions should also be provided. (Example: 16S rrna; FWD:ACGTAGCTATGACGT REV:GTGCTAGTCGAGTAC; initial denaturation:90C_5min; denaturation:90C_2min; annealing:52C_30 sec; elongation:72C_30 sec; 90 C for 1 min; final elongation:72C_5min; 30 cycles)

org_particles.name: org_particles
org_particles.title: organic particles
org_particles.description: Concentration of particles such as faeces, hairs, food, vomit, paper fibers, plant material, humus, etc

organism_count.name: organism_count
organism_count.title: organism count
organism_count.description: Total cell count of any organism (or group of organisms) per gram, volume or area of sample, should include name of organism followed by count. The method that was used for the enumeration (e.g. qPCR, atp, mpn, etc.) Should also be provided. (example: total prokaryotes; 3.5e7 cells per ml; qpcr)

otu_class_appr.name: otu_class_appr
otu_class_appr.title: OTU classification approach
otu_class_appr.description: Cutoffs and approach used when clustering species-level OTUs. Note that results from standard 95% ANI / 85% AF clustering should be provided alongside OTUs defined from another set of thresholds, even if the latter are the ones primarily used during the analysis

otu_db.name: otu_db
otu_db.title: OTU database
otu_db.description: Reference database (i.e. sequences not generated as part of the current study) used to cluster new genomes in "species-level" OTUs, if any

otu_seq_comp_appr.name: otu_seq_comp_appr
otu_seq_comp_appr.title: OTU sequence comparison approach
otu_seq_comp_appr.description: Tool and thresholds used to compare sequences when computing "species-level" OTUs

owc_tvdss.name: owc_tvdss
owc_tvdss.title: oil water contact depth
owc_tvdss.description: Depth of the original oil water contact (OWC) zone (average) (m TVDSS)

oxygen.name: oxygen
oxygen.title: oxygen
oxygen.description: Oxygen (gas) amount or concentration at the time of sampling

part_plant_animal.name: part_plant_animal
part_plant_animal.title: part of plant or animal
part_plant_animal.description: The anatomical part of the organism being involved in food production or consumption; e.g., a carrot is the root of the plant (root vegetable). This field accepts terms listed under part of plant or animal (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03420116)

particle_class.name: particle_class
particle_class.title: particle classification
particle_class.description: Particles are classified, based on their size, into six general categories:clay, silt, sand, gravel, cobbles, and boulders; should include amount of particle preceded by the name of the particle type; can include multiple values

pathogenicity.name: pathogenicity
pathogenicity.title: known pathogenicity
pathogenicity.description: To what is the entity pathogenic

pcr_cond.name: pcr_cond
pcr_cond.title: pcr conditions
pcr_cond.description: Description of reaction conditions and components of polymerase chain reaction performed during library preparation.

pcr_primers.name: pcr_primers
pcr_primers.title: pcr primers
pcr_primers.description: PCR primers that were used to amplify the sequence of the targeted gene, locus or subfragment. This field should contain all the primers used for a single PCR reaction if multiple forward or reverse primers are present in a single PCR reaction. The primer sequence should be reported in uppercase letters

permeability.name: permeability
permeability.title: permeability
permeability.description: Measure of the ability of a hydrocarbon resource to allow fluids to pass through it. (Additional information: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Permeability_(earth_sciences))

perturbation.name: perturbation
perturbation.title: perturbation
perturbation.description: Type of perturbation, e.g. chemical administration, physical disturbance, etc., coupled with perturbation regimen including how many times the perturbation was repeated, how long each perturbation lasted, and the start and end time of the entire perturbation period; can include multiple perturbation types

pesticide_regm.name: pesticide_regm
pesticide_regm.title: pesticide regimen
pesticide_regm.description: Information about treatment involving use of insecticides; should include the name of pesticide, amount administered, treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment; can include multiple pesticide regimens

pet_farm_animal.name: pet_farm_animal
pet_farm_animal.title: presence of pets or farm animals
pet_farm_animal.description: Specification of presence of pets or farm animals in the environment of subject, if yes the animals should be specified; can include multiple animals present

ph.name: ph
ph.title: pH
ph.description: pH measurement of the sample, or liquid portion of sample, or aqueous phase of the fluid

ph_meth.name: ph_meth
ph_meth.title: pH method
ph_meth.description: Reference or method used in determining pH

ph_regm.name: ph_regm
ph_regm.title: pH regimen
ph_regm.description: Information about treatment involving exposure of plants to varying levels of ph of the growth media, treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment; can include multiple regimen

phaeopigments.name: phaeopigments
phaeopigments.title: phaeopigments
phaeopigments.description: Concentration of phaeopigments; can include multiple phaeopigments

phosplipid_fatt_acid.name: phosplipid_fatt_acid
phosplipid_fatt_acid.title: phospholipid fatty acid
phosplipid_fatt_acid.description: Concentration of phospholipid fatty acids; can include multiple values

photon_flux.name: photon_flux
photon_flux.title: photon flux
examples: 3.926 micromole photons per second per square meter

photosynt_activ.name: photosynt_activ
photosynt_activ.title: photosynthetic activity
photosynt_activ.description: Measurement of photosythetic activity (i.e. leaf gas exchange / chlorophyll fluorescence emissions / reflectance / transpiration) Please also include the term method term detailing the method of activity measurement

photosynt_activ_meth.name: photosynt_activ_meth
photosynt_activ_meth.title: photosynthetic activity method
photosynt_activ_meth.description: Reference or method used in measurement of photosythetic activity

plant_growth_med.name: plant_growth_med
plant_growth_med.title: plant growth medium
plant_growth_med.description: Specification of the media for growing the plants or tissue cultured samples, e.g. soil, aeroponic, hydroponic, in vitro solid culture medium, in vitro liquid culture medium. Recommended value is a specific value from EO:plant growth medium (follow this link for terms http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/EO_0007147) or other controlled vocabulary

plant_part_maturity.name: plant_part_maturity
plant_part_maturity.title: degree of plant part maturity
plant_part_maturity.description: A description of the stage of development of a plant or plant part based on maturity or ripeness. This field accepts terms listed under degree of plant maturity (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03530050)

plant_product.name: plant_product
plant_product.title: plant product
plant_product.description: Substance produced by the plant, where the sample was obtained from

plant_reprod_crop.name: plant_reprod_crop
plant_reprod_crop.title: plant reproductive part
plant_reprod_crop.description: Plant reproductive part used in the field during planting to start the crop

plant_sex.name: plant_sex
plant_sex.title: plant sex
plant_sex.description: Sex of the reproductive parts on the whole plant, e.g. pistillate, staminate, monoecieous, hermaphrodite

plant_struc.name: plant_struc
plant_struc.title: plant structure
plant_struc.description: Name of plant structure the sample was obtained from; for Plant Ontology (PO) (v releases/2017-12-14) terms, see http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/PO, e.g. petiole epidermis (PO_0000051). If an individual flower is sampled, the sex of it can be recorded here

plant_water_method.name: plant_water_method
plant_water_method.title: plant water delivery method
plant_water_method.description: Description of the equipment or method used to distribute water to crops. This field accepts termed listed under irrigation process (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/AGRO_00000006). Multiple terms can be separated by pipes

ploidy.name: ploidy
ploidy.title: ploidy
ploidy.description: The ploidy level of the genome (e.g. allopolyploid, haploid, diploid, triploid, tetraploid). It has implications for the downstream study of duplicated gene and regions of the genomes (and perhaps for difficulties in assembly). For terms, please select terms listed under class ploidy (PATO:001374) of Phenotypic Quality Ontology (PATO), and for a browser of PATO (v 2018-03-27) please refer to http://purl.bioontology.org/ontology/PATO

pollutants.name: pollutants
pollutants.title: pollutants
pollutants.description: Pollutant types and, amount or concentrations measured at the time of sampling; can report multiple pollutants by entering numeric values preceded by name of pollutant

pool_dna_extracts.name: pool_dna_extracts
pool_dna_extracts.title: pooling of DNA extracts (if done)
pool_dna_extracts.description: Indicate whether multiple DNA extractions were mixed. If the answer yes, the number of extracts that were pooled should be given

porosity.name: porosity
porosity.title: porosity
porosity.description: Porosity of deposited sediment is volume of voids divided by the total volume of sample

pos_cont_type.name: pos_cont_type
pos_cont_type.title: positive control type
pos_cont_type.description: The substance, mixture, product, or apparatus used to verify that a process which is part of an investigation delivers a true positive

potassium.name: potassium
potassium.title: potassium
potassium.description: Concentration of potassium in the sample

pour_point.name: pour_point
pour_point.title: pour point
pour_point.description: Temperature at which a liquid becomes semi solid and loses its flow characteristics. In crude oil a high pour point is generally associated with a high paraffin content, typically found in crude deriving from a larger proportion of plant material. (source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/pour_point)

pre_treatment.name: pre_treatment
pre_treatment.title: pre-treatment
pre_treatment.description: The process of pre-treatment removes materials that can be easily collected from the raw wastewater

pred_genome_struc.name: pred_genome_struc
pred_genome_struc.title: predicted genome structure
pred_genome_struc.description: Expected structure of the viral genome

pred_genome_type.name: pred_genome_type
pred_genome_type.title: predicted genome type
pred_genome_type.description: Type of genome predicted for the UViG

pres_animal_insect.name: pres_animal_insect
pres_animal_insect.title: presence of pets, animals, or insects
pres_animal_insect.description: The type and number of animals or insects present in the sampling space

pressure.name: pressure
pressure.title: pressure
pressure.description: Pressure to which the sample is subject to, in atmospheres

prev_land_use_meth.name: prev_land_use_meth
prev_land_use_meth.title: history/previous land use method
prev_land_use_meth.description: Reference or method used in determining previous land use and dates

primary_prod.name: primary_prod
primary_prod.title: primary production
primary_prod.description: Measurement of primary production, generally measured as isotope uptake
examples: 100 milligram per cubic meter per day

primary_treatment.name: primary_treatment
primary_treatment.title: primary treatment
primary_treatment.description: The process to produce both a generally homogeneous liquid capable of being treated biologically and a sludge that can be separately treated or processed

prod_label_claims.name: prod_label_claims
prod_label_claims.title: production labeling claims
prod_label_claims.description: Labeling claims containing descriptors such as wild caught, free-range, organic, free-range, industrial, hormone-free, antibiotic free, cage free. Can include more than one term, separated by ";"

prod_rate.name: prod_rate
prod_rate.title: production rate
prod_rate.description: Oil and/or gas production rates per well (e.g. 524 m3 / day)

profile_position.name: profile_position
profile_position.title: profile position
profile_position.description: Cross-sectional position in the hillslope where sample was collected.sample area position in relation to surrounding areas

project_name.name: project_name
project_name.title: project name
project_name.description: Name of the project within which the sequencing was organized

propagation.name: propagation
propagation.title: propagation
propagation.description: The type of reproduction from the parent stock. Values for this field is specific to different taxa. For phage or virus: lytic/lysogenic/temperate/obligately lytic. For plasmids: incompatibility group. For eukaryotes: sexual/asexual

pulmonary_disord.name: pulmonary_disord
pulmonary_disord.title: lung/pulmonary disorder
pulmonary_disord.description: History of pulmonary disorders; can include multiple disorders. The terms should be chosen from the DO (Human Disease Ontology) at http://www.disease-ontology.org, lung disease (https://disease-ontology.org/?id=DOID:850)

quad_pos.name: quad_pos
quad_pos.title: quadrant position
quad_pos.description: The quadrant position of the sampling room within the building

radiation_regm.name: radiation_regm
radiation_regm.title: radiation regimen
radiation_regm.description: Information about treatment involving exposure of plant or a plant part to a particular radiation regimen; should include the radiation type, amount or intensity administered, treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment; can include multiple radiation regimens

rainfall_regm.name: rainfall_regm
rainfall_regm.title: rainfall regimen
rainfall_regm.description: Information about treatment involving an exposure to a given amount of rainfall, treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment; can include multiple regimens

reactor_type.name: reactor_type
reactor_type.title: reactor type
reactor_type.description: Anaerobic digesters can be designed and engineered to operate using a number of different process configurations, as batch or continuous, mesophilic, high solid or low solid, and single stage or multistage

reassembly_bin.name: reassembly_bin
reassembly_bin.title: reassembly post binning
reassembly_bin.description: Has an assembly been performed on a genome bin extracted from a metagenomic assembly?

redox_potential.name: redox_potential
redox_potential.title: redox potential
redox_potential.description: Redox potential, measured relative to a hydrogen cell, indicating oxidation or reduction potential

ref_biomaterial.name: ref_biomaterial
ref_biomaterial.title: reference for biomaterial
ref_biomaterial.description: Primary publication if isolated before genome publication; otherwise, primary genome report

ref_db.name: ref_db
ref_db.title: reference database(s)
ref_db.description: List of database(s) used for ORF annotation, along with version number and reference to website or publication

rel_air_humidity.name: rel_air_humidity
rel_air_humidity.title: relative air humidity
rel_air_humidity.description: Partial vapor and air pressure, density of the vapor and air, or by the actual mass of the vapor and air

rel_humidity_out.name: rel_humidity_out
rel_humidity_out.title: outside relative humidity
rel_humidity_out.description: The recorded outside relative humidity value at the time of sampling

rel_location.name: rel_location
rel_location.title: relative location of sample
rel_location.description: Location of sampled soil to other parts of the farm e.g. under crop plant, near irrigation ditch, from the dirt road

rel_samp_loc.name: rel_samp_loc
rel_samp_loc.title: relative sampling location
rel_samp_loc.description: The sampling location within the train car

rel_to_oxygen.name: rel_to_oxygen
rel_to_oxygen.title: relationship to oxygen
rel_to_oxygen.description: Is this organism an aerobe, anaerobe? Please note that aerobic and anaerobic are valid descriptors for microbial environments

repository_name.name: repository_name
repository_name.title: repository name
repository_name.description: The name of the institution where the sample or DNA extract is held or "sample not available" if the sample was used in its entirety for analysis or otherwise not retained

resins_pc.name: resins_pc
resins_pc.title: resins wt%
resins_pc.description: Saturate, Aromatic, Resin and Asphaltene (SARA) is an analysis method that divides crude oil components according to their polarizability and polarity. There are three main methods to obtain SARA results. The most popular one is known as the Iatroscan TLC-FID and is referred to as IP-143 (source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saturate,_aromatic,_resin_and_asphaltene)

room_air_exch_rate.name: room_air_exch_rate
room_air_exch_rate.title: room air exchange rate
room_air_exch_rate.description: The rate at which outside air replaces indoor air in a given space

room_architec_elem.name: room_architec_elem
room_architec_elem.title: room architectural elements
room_architec_elem.description: The unique details and component parts that, together, form the architecture of a distinguishable space within a built structure

room_condt.name: room_condt
room_condt.title: room condition
room_condt.description: The condition of the room at the time of sampling

room_connected.name: room_connected
room_connected.title: rooms connected by a doorway
room_connected.description: List of rooms connected to the sampling room by a doorway

room_count.name: room_count
room_count.title: room count
room_count.description: The total count of rooms in the built structure including all room types

room_dim.name: room_dim
room_dim.title: room dimensions
room_dim.description: The length, width and height of sampling room

room_door_dist.name: room_door_dist
room_door_dist.title: room door distance
room_door_dist.description: Distance between doors (meters) in the hallway between the sampling room and adjacent rooms

room_door_share.name: room_door_share
room_door_share.title: rooms that share a door with sampling room
room_door_share.description: List of room(s) (room number, room name) sharing a door with the sampling room

room_hallway.name: room_hallway
room_hallway.title: rooms that are on the same hallway
room_hallway.description: List of room(s) (room number, room name) located in the same hallway as sampling room

room_loc.name: room_loc
room_loc.title: room location in building
room_loc.description: The position of the room within the building

room_moist_dam_hist.name: room_moist_dam_hist
room_moist_dam_hist.title: room moisture damage or mold history
room_moist_dam_hist.description: The history of moisture damage or mold in the past 12 months. Number of events of moisture damage or mold observed

room_net_area.name: room_net_area
room_net_area.title: room net area
room_net_area.description: The net floor area of sampling room. Net area excludes wall thicknesses

room_occup.name: room_occup
room_occup.title: room occupancy
room_occup.description: Count of room occupancy at time of sampling

room_samp_pos.name: room_samp_pos
room_samp_pos.title: room sampling position
room_samp_pos.description: The horizontal sampling position in the room relative to architectural elements

room_type.name: room_type
room_type.title: room type
room_type.description: The main purpose or activity of the sampling room. A room is any distinguishable space within a structure

room_wall_share.name: room_wall_share
room_wall_share.title: rooms that share a wall with sampling room
room_wall_share.description: List of room(s) (room number, room name) sharing a wall with the sampling room

room_window_count.name: room_window_count
room_window_count.title: room window count
room_window_count.description: Number of windows in the room

root_cond.name: root_cond
root_cond.title: rooting conditions
root_cond.description: Relevant rooting conditions such as field plot size, sowing density, container dimensions, number of plants per container

root_med_carbon.name: root_med_carbon
root_med_carbon.title: rooting medium carbon
root_med_carbon.description: Source of organic carbon in the culture rooting medium; e.g. sucrose

root_med_macronutr.name: root_med_macronutr
root_med_macronutr.title: rooting medium macronutrients
root_med_macronutr.description: Measurement of the culture rooting medium macronutrients (N,P, K, Ca, Mg, S); e.g. KH2PO4 (170 mg/L)

root_med_micronutr.name: root_med_micronutr
root_med_micronutr.title: rooting medium micronutrients
root_med_micronutr.description: Measurement of the culture rooting medium micronutrients (Fe, Mn, Zn, B, Cu, Mo); e.g. H3BO3 (6.2 mg/L)

root_med_ph.name: root_med_ph
root_med_ph.title: rooting medium pH
root_med_ph.description: pH measurement of the culture rooting medium; e.g. 5.5

root_med_regl.name: root_med_regl
root_med_regl.title: rooting medium regulators
root_med_regl.description: Growth regulators in the culture rooting medium such as cytokinins, auxins, gybberellins, abscisic acid; e.g. 0.5 mg/L NAA

root_med_solid.name: root_med_solid
root_med_solid.title: rooting medium solidifier
root_med_solid.description: Specification of the solidifying agent in the culture rooting medium; e.g. agar

root_med_suppl.name: root_med_suppl
root_med_suppl.title: rooting medium organic supplements
root_med_suppl.description: Organic supplements of the culture rooting medium, such as vitamins, amino acids, organic acids, antibiotics activated charcoal; e.g. nicotinic acid (0.5 mg/L)

route_transmission.name: route_transmission
route_transmission.title: route of transmission
route_transmission.description: Description of path taken by the symbiotic host organism being sampled in order to establish a symbiotic relationship with the host (with which it was observed at the time of sampling) via a mode of transmission (specified in mode_transmission)

salinity.name: salinity
salinity.title: salinity
salinity.description: The total concentration of all dissolved salts in a liquid or solid sample. While salinity can be measured by a complete chemical analysis, this method is difficult and time consuming. More often, it is instead derived from the conductivity measurement. This is known as practical salinity. These derivations compare the specific conductance of the sample to a salinity standard such as seawater

salt_regm.name: salt_regm
salt_regm.title: salt regimen
salt_regm.description: Information about treatment involving use of salts as supplement to liquid and soil growth media; should include the name of salt, amount administered, treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment; can include multiple salt regimens

samp_collect_device.name: samp_collect_device
samp_collect_device.title: sample collection device
samp_collect_device.description: The device used to collect an environmental sample. This field accepts terms listed under environmental sampling device (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO). This field also accepts terms listed under specimen collection device (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GENEPIO_0002094)

samp_collect_method.name: samp_collect_method
samp_collect_method.title: sample collection method
samp_collect_method.description: The method employed for collecting the sample

samp_collect_point.name: samp_collect_point
samp_collect_point.title: sample collection point
samp_collect_point.description: Sampling point on the asset were sample was collected (e.g. Wellhead, storage tank, separator, etc). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

samp_dis_stage.name: samp_dis_stage
samp_dis_stage.title: sample disease stage
samp_dis_stage.description: Stage of the disease at the time of sample collection, e.g. inoculation, penetration, infection, growth and reproduction, dissemination of pathogen

samp_floor.name: samp_floor
samp_floor.title: sampling floor
samp_floor.description: The floor of the building, where the sampling room is located

samp_loc_condition.name: samp_loc_condition
samp_loc_condition.title: sample location condition
samp_loc_condition.description: The condition of the sample location at the time of sampling

samp_loc_corr_rate.name: samp_loc_corr_rate
samp_loc_corr_rate.title: corrosion rate at sample location
samp_loc_corr_rate.description: Metal corrosion rate is the speed of metal deterioration due to environmental conditions. As environmental conditions change corrosion rates change accordingly. Therefore, long term corrosion rates are generally more informative than short term rates and for that reason they are preferred during reporting. In the case of suspected MIC, corrosion rate measurements at the time of sampling might provide insights into the involvement of certain microbial community members in MIC as well as potential microbial interplays

samp_mat_process.name: samp_mat_process
samp_mat_process.title: sample material processing
samp_mat_process.description: A brief description of any processing applied to the sample during or after retrieving the sample from environment, or a link to the relevant protocol(s) performed
examples: filtering of seawater, storing samples in ethanol

samp_md.name: samp_md
samp_md.title: sample measured depth
samp_md.description: In non deviated well, measured depth is equal to the true vertical depth, TVD (TVD=TVDSS plus the reference or datum it refers to). In deviated wells, the MD is the length of trajectory of the borehole measured from the same reference or datum. Common datums used are ground level (GL), drilling rig floor (DF), rotary table (RT), kelly bushing (KB) and mean sea level (MSL). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

samp_name.name: samp_name
samp_name.title: sample name
samp_name.description: A local identifier or name that for the material sample used for extracting nucleic acids, and subsequent sequencing. It can refer either to the original material collected or to any derived sub-samples. It can have any format, but we suggest that you make it concise, unique and consistent within your lab, and as informative as possible. INSDC requires every sample name from a single Submitter to be unique. Use of a globally unique identifier for the field source_mat_id is recommended in addition to sample_name

samp_pooling.name: samp_pooling
samp_pooling.title: sample pooling
samp_pooling.description: Physical combination of several instances of like material, e.g. RNA extracted from samples or dishes of cell cultures into one big aliquot of cells. Please provide a short description of the samples that were pooled
examples: 5uL of extracted genomic DNA from 5 leaves were pooled

samp_preserv.name: samp_preserv
samp_preserv.title: preservative added to sample
samp_preserv.description: Preservative added to the sample (e.g. Rnalater, alcohol, formaldehyde, etc.). Where appropriate include volume added (e.g. Rnalater; 2 ml)

samp_purpose.name: samp_purpose
samp_purpose.title: purpose of sampling
samp_purpose.description: The reason that the sample was collected

samp_rep_biol.name: samp_rep_biol
samp_rep_biol.title: biological sample replicate
samp_rep_biol.description: Measurements of biologically distinct samples that show biological variation

samp_rep_tech.name: samp_rep_tech
samp_rep_tech.title: technical sample replicate
samp_rep_tech.description: Repeated measurements of the same sample that show independent measures of the noise associated with the equipment and the protocols

samp_room_id.name: samp_room_id
samp_room_id.title: sampling room ID or name
samp_room_id.description: Sampling room number. This ID should be consistent with the designations on the building floor plans

samp_size.name: samp_size
samp_size.title: amount or size of sample collected
samp_size.description: The total amount or size (volume (ml), mass (g) or area (m2) ) of sample collected

samp_sort_meth.name: samp_sort_meth
samp_sort_meth.title: sample size sorting method
samp_sort_meth.description: Method by which samples are sorted; open face filter collecting total suspended particles, prefilter to remove particles larger than X micrometers in diameter, where common values of X would be 10 and 2.5 full size sorting in a cascade impactor

samp_source_mat_cat.name: samp_source_mat_cat
samp_source_mat_cat.title: sample source material category
samp_source_mat_cat.description: This is the scientific role or category that the subject organism or material has with respect to an investigation. This field accepts terms listed under specimen source material category (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GENEPIO_0001237 or http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0100051)

samp_stor_device.name: samp_stor_device
samp_stor_device.title: sample storage device
samp_stor_device.description: The container used to store the sample. This field accepts terms listed under container (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCIT_C43186). If the proper descriptor is not listed please use text to describe the storage device

samp_stor_media.name: samp_stor_media
samp_stor_media.title: sample storage media
samp_stor_media.description: The liquid that is added to the sample collection device prior to sampling. If the sample is pre-hydrated, indicate the liquid media the sample is pre-hydrated with for storage purposes. This field accepts terms listed under microbiological culture medium (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/MICRO_0000067). If the proper descriptor is not listed please use text to describe the sample storage media

samp_store_dur.name: samp_store_dur
samp_store_dur.title: sample storage duration
samp_store_dur.description: Duration for which the sample was stored. Indicate the duration for which the sample was stored written in ISO 8601 format

samp_store_loc.name: samp_store_loc
samp_store_loc.title: sample storage location
samp_store_loc.description: Location at which sample was stored, usually name of a specific freezer/room

samp_store_sol.name: samp_store_sol
samp_store_sol.title: sample storage solution
samp_store_sol.description: Solution within which sample was stored, if any

samp_store_temp.name: samp_store_temp
samp_store_temp.title: sample storage temperature
samp_store_temp.description: Temperature at which sample was stored, e.g. -80 degree Celsius

samp_subtype.name: samp_subtype
samp_subtype.title: sample subtype
samp_subtype.description: Name of sample sub-type. For example if "sample type" is "Produced Water" then subtype could be "Oil Phase" or "Water Phase". If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

samp_surf_moisture.name: samp_surf_moisture
samp_surf_moisture.title: sample surface moisture
samp_surf_moisture.description: Degree of water held on a sampled surface. If present, user can state the degree of water held on surface (intermittent moisture, submerged). If no surface moisture is present indicate not present

samp_taxon_id.name: samp_taxon_id
samp_taxon_id.title: taxonomy ID of DNA sample
samp_taxon_id.description: NCBI taxon id of the sample. Maybe be a single taxon or mixed taxa sample. Use 'synthetic metagenome for mock community/positive controls, or 'blank sample' for negative controls

samp_time_out.name: samp_time_out
samp_time_out.title: sampling time outside
samp_time_out.description: The recent and long term history of outside sampling

samp_transport_cond.name: samp_transport_cond
samp_transport_cond.title: sample transport conditions
samp_transport_cond.description: Sample transport duration (in days or hrs) and temperature the sample was exposed to (e.g. 5.5 days; 20 C)

samp_transport_cont.name: samp_transport_cont
samp_transport_cont.title: sample transport container
samp_transport_cont.description: Container in which the sample was stored during transport. Indicate the location name

samp_transport_dur.name: samp_transport_dur
samp_transport_dur.title: sample transport duration
samp_transport_dur.description: The duration of time from when the sample was collected until processed. Indicate the duration for which the sample was stored written in ISO 8601 format

samp_transport_temp.name: samp_transport_temp
samp_transport_temp.title: sample transport temperature
samp_transport_temp.description: Temperature at which sample was transported, e.g. -20 or 4 degree Celsius

samp_tvdss.name: samp_tvdss
samp_tvdss.title: sample true vertical depth subsea
samp_tvdss.description: Depth of the sample i.e. The vertical distance between the sea level and the sampled position in the subsurface. Depth can be reported as an interval for subsurface samples e.g. 1325.75-1362.25 m

samp_type.name: samp_type
samp_type.title: sample type
samp_type.description: The type of material from which the sample was obtained. For the Hydrocarbon package, samples include types like core, rock trimmings, drill cuttings, piping section, coupon, pigging debris, solid deposit, produced fluid, produced water, injected water, swabs, etc. For the Food Package, samples are usually categorized as food, body products or tissues, or environmental material. This field accepts terms listed under environmental specimen (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GENEPIO_0001246)

samp_vol_we_dna_ext.name: samp_vol_we_dna_ext
samp_vol_we_dna_ext.title: sample volume or weight for DNA extraction
samp_vol_we_dna_ext.description: Volume (ml) or mass (g) of total collected sample processed for DNA extraction. Note: total sample collected should be entered under the term Sample Size (MIXS:0000001)

samp_weather.name: samp_weather
samp_weather.title: sampling day weather
samp_weather.description: The weather on the sampling day

samp_well_name.name: samp_well_name
samp_well_name.title: sample well name
samp_well_name.description: Name of the well (e.g. BXA1123) where sample was taken

saturates_pc.name: saturates_pc
saturates_pc.title: saturates wt%
saturates_pc.description: Saturate, Aromatic, Resin and Asphaltene (SARA) is an analysis method that divides crude oil components according to their polarizability and polarity. There are three main methods to obtain SARA results. The most popular one is known as the Iatroscan TLC-FID and is referred to as IP-143 (source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saturate,_aromatic,_resin_and_asphaltene)

sc_lysis_approach.name: sc_lysis_approach
sc_lysis_approach.title: single cell or viral particle lysis approach
sc_lysis_approach.description: Method used to free DNA from interior of the cell(s) or particle(s)

sc_lysis_method.name: sc_lysis_method
sc_lysis_method.title: single cell or viral particle lysis kit protocol
sc_lysis_method.description: Name of the kit or standard protocol used for cell(s) or particle(s) lysis

season.name: season
season.title: season
season.description: The season when sampling occurred. Any of the four periods into which the year is divided by the equinoxes and solstices. This field accepts terms listed under season (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCIT_C94729)

season_environment.name: season_environment
season_environment.title: seasonal environment
season_environment.description: Treatment involving an exposure to a particular season (e.g. Winter, summer, rabi, rainy etc.), treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment

season_humidity.name: season_humidity
season_humidity.title: mean seasonal humidity
season_humidity.description: Average humidity of the region throughout the growing season

season_precpt.name: season_precpt
season_precpt.title: mean seasonal precipitation
season_precpt.description: The average of all seasonal precipitation values known, or an estimated equivalent value derived by such methods as regional indexes or Isohyetal maps

season_use.name: season_use
season_use.title: seasonal use
season_use.description: The seasons the space is occupied

secondary_treatment.name: secondary_treatment
secondary_treatment.title: secondary treatment
secondary_treatment.description: The process for substantially degrading the biological content of the sewage

sediment_type.name: sediment_type
sediment_type.title: sediment type
sediment_type.description: Information about the sediment type based on major constituents

seq_meth.name: seq_meth
seq_meth.title: sequencing method
seq_meth.description: Sequencing machine used. Where possible the term should be taken from the OBI list of DNA sequencers (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0400103)

seq_quality_check.name: seq_quality_check
seq_quality_check.title: sequence quality check
seq_quality_check.description: Indicate if the sequence has been called by automatic systems (none) or undergone a manual editing procedure (e.g. by inspecting the raw data or chromatograms). Applied only for sequences that are not submitted to SRA,ENA or DRA

sequencing_kit.name: sequencing_kit
sequencing_kit.title: sequencing kit
sequencing_kit.description: Pre-filled, ready-to-use reagent cartridges. Used to produce improved chemistry, cluster density and read length as well as improve quality (Q) scores. Reagent components are encoded to interact with the sequencing system to validate compatibility with user-defined applications. Indicate name of the sequencing kit
examples: NextSeq 500/550 High Output Kit v2.5 (75 Cycles)

sequencing_location.name: sequencing_location
sequencing_location.title: sequencing location
sequencing_location.description: The location the sequencing run was performed. Indicate the name of the lab or core facility where samples were sequenced
examples: University of Maryland Genomics Resource Center

serovar_or_serotype.name: serovar_or_serotype
serovar_or_serotype.title: serovar or serotype
serovar_or_serotype.description: A characterization of a cell or microorganism based on the antigenic properties of the molecules on its surface. Indicate the name of a serovar or serotype of interest. This field accepts terms under organism (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCIT_C14250). This field also accepts identification numbers from NCBI under https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/taxonomy

sewage_type.name: sewage_type
sewage_type.title: sewage type
sewage_type.description: Type of wastewater treatment plant as municipial or industrial

sexual_act.name: sexual_act
sexual_act.title: sexual activity
sexual_act.description: Current sexual partner and frequency of sex

shad_dev_water_mold.name: shad_dev_water_mold
shad_dev_water_mold.title: shading device signs of water/mold
shad_dev_water_mold.description: Signs of the presence of mold or mildew on the shading device

shading_device_cond.name: shading_device_cond
shading_device_cond.title: shading device condition
shading_device_cond.description: The physical condition of the shading device at the time of sampling

shading_device_loc.name: shading_device_loc
shading_device_loc.title: shading device location
shading_device_loc.description: The location of the shading device in relation to the built structure

shading_device_mat.name: shading_device_mat
shading_device_mat.title: shading device material
shading_device_mat.description: The material the shading device is composed of

sieving.name: sieving
sieving.title: sieving
sieving.description: Collection design of pooled samples and/or sieve size and amount of sample sieved

sim_search_meth.name: sim_search_meth
sim_search_meth.title: similarity search method
sim_search_meth.description: Tool used to compare ORFs with database, along with version and cutoffs used

size_frac.name: size_frac
size_frac.title: size fraction selected
size_frac.description: Filtering pore size used in sample preparation

size_frac_low.name: size_frac_low
size_frac_low.title: size-fraction lower threshold
size_frac_low.description: Refers to the mesh/pore size used to pre-filter/pre-sort the sample. Materials smaller than the size threshold are excluded from the sample

size_frac_up.name: size_frac_up
size_frac_up.title: size-fraction upper threshold
size_frac_up.description: Mesh or pore size of the device used to retain the sample. Materials larger than the size threshold are excluded from the sample.

slope_aspect.name: slope_aspect
slope_aspect.title: slope aspect
slope_aspect.description: The direction a slope faces. While looking down a slope use a compass to record the direction you are facing (direction or degrees); e.g., nw or 315 degrees. This measure provides an indication of sun and wind exposure that will influence soil temperature and evapotranspiration

slope_gradient.name: slope_gradient
slope_gradient.title: slope gradient
slope_gradient.description: Commonly called 'slope'. The angle between ground surface and a horizontal line (in percent). This is the direction that overland water would flow. This measure is usually taken with a hand level meter or clinometer

sludge_retent_time.name: sludge_retent_time
sludge_retent_time.title: sludge retention time
sludge_retent_time.description: The time activated sludge remains in reactor

soil_conductivity.name: soil_conductivity
soil_conductivity.title: soil conductivity
comments: Soil electrical conductivity (EC) is a measure of how well soil water can carry an electrical current. Electrical conductivity of soil solution is a reliable indicator of its solute (cation or anion) concentration with 1 dS/m approximately equivalent to 10 meq/L (U.S. Salinity Laboratory Staff, 1954).

soil_cover.name: soil_cover
soil_cover.title: soil cover
soil_cover.description: Material covering the sampled soil. This field accepts terms under ENVO:00010483, environmental material

soil_horizon.name: soil_horizon
soil_horizon.title: soil horizon
soil_horizon.description: Specific layer in the land area which measures parallel to the soil surface and possesses physical characteristics which differ from the layers above and beneath

soil_pH.name: soil_pH
soil_pH.title: soil pH
soil_pH.description: pH measurement of some soil at the time of sampling

soil_porosity.name: soil_porosity
soil_porosity.title: soil sediment porosity
soil_porosity.description: Porosity of soil or deposited sediment is volume of voids divided by the total volume of sample

soil_temp.name: soil_temp
soil_temp.title: soil temperature
soil_temp.description: Temperature of soil at the time of sampling

soil_texture.name: soil_texture
soil_texture.title: soil texture
soil_texture.description: The relative proportion of different grain sizes of mineral particles in a soil, as described using a standard system; express as % sand (50 um to 2 mm), silt (2 um to 50 um), and clay (<2 um) with textural name (e.g., silty clay loam) optional

soil_texture_class.name: soil_texture_class
soil_texture_class.title: soil texture classification
soil_texture_class.description: One of the 12 soil texture classes use to describe soil texture based on the relative proportion of different grain sizes of mineral particles [sand (50 um to 2 mm), silt (2 um to 50 um), and clay (<2 um)] in a soil

soil_texture_meth.name: soil_texture_meth
soil_texture_meth.title: soil texture method
soil_texture_meth.description: Reference or method used in determining soil texture

soil_type.name: soil_type
soil_type.title: soil type
soil_type.description: Description of the soil type or classification. This field accepts terms under soil (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_00001998). Multiple terms can be separated by pipes

soil_type_meth.name: soil_type_meth
soil_type_meth.title: soil type method
soil_type_meth.description: Reference or method used in determining soil series name or other lower-level classification

solar_irradiance.name: solar_irradiance
solar_irradiance.title: solar irradiance
solar_irradiance.description: The amount of solar energy that arrives at a specific area of a surface during a specific time interval
examples: 1.36 kilowatts per square meter per day

soluble_inorg_mat.name: soluble_inorg_mat
soluble_inorg_mat.title: soluble inorganic material
soluble_inorg_mat.description: Concentration of substances such as ammonia, road-salt, sea-salt, cyanide, hydrogen sulfide, thiocyanates, thiosulfates, etc

soluble_org_mat.name: soluble_org_mat
soluble_org_mat.title: soluble organic material
soluble_org_mat.description: Concentration of substances such as urea, fruit sugars, soluble proteins, drugs, pharmaceuticals, etc

sop.name: sop
sop.title: relevant standard operating procedures
sop.description: Standard operating procedures used in assembly and/or annotation of genomes, metagenomes or environmental sequences

sort_tech.name: sort_tech
sort_tech.title: sorting technology
sort_tech.description: Method used to sort/isolate cells or particles of interest

source_mat_id.name: source_mat_id
source_mat_id.title: source material identifiers
source_mat_id.description: A unique identifier assigned to a material sample (as defined by http://rs.tdwg.org/dwc/terms/materialSampleID, and as opposed to a particular digital record of a material sample) used for extracting nucleic acids, and subsequent sequencing. The identifier can refer either to the original material collected or to any derived sub-samples. The INSDC qualifiers /specimen_voucher, /bio_material, or /culture_collection may or may not share the same value as the source_mat_id field. For instance, the /specimen_voucher qualifier and source_mat_id may both contain 'UAM:Herps:14' , referring to both the specimen voucher and sampled tissue with the same identifier. However, the /culture_collection qualifier may refer to a value from an initial culture (e.g. ATCC:11775) while source_mat_id would refer to an identifier from some derived culture from which the nucleic acids were extracted (e.g. xatc123 or ark:/2154/R2)

source_uvig.name: source_uvig
source_uvig.title: source of UViGs
source_uvig.description: Type of dataset from which the UViG was obtained

space_typ_state.name: space_typ_state
space_typ_state.title: space typical state
space_typ_state.description: Customary or normal state of the space

spec_intended_cons.name: spec_intended_cons
spec_intended_cons.title: specific intended consumer
spec_intended_cons.description: Food consumer type, human or animal, for which the food product is produced and marketed. This field accepts terms listed under food consumer group (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_03510136)

special_diet.name: special_diet
special_diet.title: special diet
special_diet.description: Specification of special diet; can include multiple special diets

specific.name: specific
specific.title: specifications
specific.description: The building specifications. If design is chosen, indicate phase: conceptual, schematic, design development, construction documents

specific_host.name: specific_host
specific_host.title: host scientific name
specific_host.description: Report the host's taxonomic name and/or NCBI taxonomy ID

specific_humidity.name: specific_humidity
specific_humidity.title: specific humidity
specific_humidity.description: The mass of water vapour in a unit mass of moist air, usually expressed as grams of vapour per kilogram of air, or, in air conditioning, as grains per pound

spikein_AMR.name: spikein_AMR
spikein_AMR.title: antimicrobial phenotype of spike-in bacteria
spikein_AMR.description: Qualitative description of a microbial response to antimicrobial agents. Bacteria may be susceptible or resistant to a broad range of antibiotic drugs or drug classes, with several intermediate states or phases. This field accepts terms under antimicrobial phenotype (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ARO_3004299)

spikein_antibiotic.name: spikein_antibiotic
spikein_antibiotic.title: spike-in with antibiotics
spikein_antibiotic.description: Antimicrobials used in research study to assess effects of exposure on microbiome of a specific site. Please list antimicrobial, common name and/or class and concentration used for spike-in

spikein_count.name: spikein_count
spikein_count.title: spike-in organism count
spikein_count.description: Total cell count of any organism (or group of organisms) per gram, volume or area of sample, should include name of organism followed by count. The method that was used for the enumeration (e.g. qPCR, atp, mpn, etc.) should also be provided (example: total prokaryotes; 3.5e7 cells per ml; qPCR)
examples: total prokaryotes;3.5e7 colony forming units per milliliter;qPCR

spikein_growth_med.name: spikein_growth_med
spikein_growth_med.title: spike-in growth medium
spikein_growth_med.description: A liquid or gel containing nutrients, salts, and other factors formulated to support the growth of microorganisms, cells, or plants (National Cancer Institute Thesaurus). A growth medium is a culture medium which has the disposition to encourage growth of particular bacteria to the exclusion of others in the same growth environment. In this case, list the culture medium used to propagate the spike-in bacteria during preparation of spike-in inoculum. This field accepts terms listed under microbiological culture medium (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/MICRO_0000067). If the proper descriptor is not listed please use text to describe the spike in growth media

spikein_metal.name: spikein_metal
spikein_metal.title: spike-in with heavy metals
spikein_metal.description: Heavy metals used in research study to assess effects of exposure on microbiome of a specific site. Please list heavy metals and concentration used for spike-in

spikein_org.name: spikein_org
spikein_org.title: spike in organism
spikein_org.description: Taxonomic information about the spike-in organism(s). This field accepts terms under organism (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCIT_C14250). This field also accepts identification numbers from NCBI under https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/taxonomy. Multiple terms can be separated by pipes

spikein_serovar.name: spikein_serovar
spikein_serovar.title: spike-in bacterial serovar or serotype
spikein_serovar.description: Taxonomic information about the spike-in organism(s) at the serovar or serotype level. This field accepts terms under organism (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCIT_C14250). This field also accepts identification numbers from NCBI under https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/taxonomy. Multiple terms can be separated by pipes

spikein_strain.name: spikein_strain
spikein_strain.title: spike-in microbial strain
spikein_strain.description: Taxonomic information about the spike-in organism(s) at the strain level. This field accepts terms under organism (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCIT_C14250). This field also accepts identification numbers from NCBI under https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/taxonomy. Multiple terms can be separated by pipes

sr_dep_env.name: sr_dep_env
sr_dep_env.title: source rock depositional environment
sr_dep_env.description: Source rock depositional environment (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Source_rock). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

sr_geol_age.name: sr_geol_age
sr_geol_age.title: source rock geological age
sr_geol_age.description: Geological age of source rock (Additional info: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Period_(geology)). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

sr_kerog_type.name: sr_kerog_type
sr_kerog_type.title: source rock kerogen type
sr_kerog_type.description: Origin of kerogen. Type I: Algal (aquatic), Type II: planktonic and soft plant material (aquatic or terrestrial), Type III: terrestrial woody/ fibrous plant material (terrestrial), Type IV: oxidized recycled woody debris (terrestrial) (additional information: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kerogen). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

sr_lithology.name: sr_lithology
sr_lithology.title: source rock lithology
sr_lithology.description: Lithology of source rock (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Source_rock). If "other" is specified, please propose entry in "additional info" field

standing_water_regm.name: standing_water_regm
standing_water_regm.title: standing water regimen
standing_water_regm.description: Treatment involving an exposure to standing water during a plant's life span, types can be flood water or standing water, treatment regimen including how many times the treatment was repeated, how long each treatment lasted, and the start and end time of the entire treatment; can include multiple regimens

ster_meth_samp_room.name: ster_meth_samp_room
ster_meth_samp_room.title: sampling room sterilization method
ster_meth_samp_room.description: The method used to sterilize the sampling room. This field accepts terms listed under electromagnetic radiation (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ENVO_01001026). If the proper descriptor is not listed, please use text to describe the sampling room sterilization method. Multiple terms can be separated by pipes

store_cond.name: store_cond
store_cond.title: storage conditions
store_cond.description: Explain how and for how long the soil sample was stored before DNA extraction (fresh/frozen/other)

study_complt_stat.name: study_complt_stat
study_complt_stat.title: study completion status
study_complt_stat.description: Specification of study completion status, if no the reason should be specified

study_design.name: study_design
study_design.title: study design
study_design.description: A plan specification comprised of protocols (which may specify how and what kinds of data will be gathered) that are executed as part of an investigation and is realized during a study design execution. This field accepts terms under study design (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OBI_0500000). If the proper descriptor is not listed please use text to describe the study design. Multiple terms can be separated by pipes

study_inc_dur.name: study_inc_dur
study_inc_dur.title: study incubation duration
study_inc_dur.description: Sample incubation duration if unpublished or unvalidated method is used. Indicate the timepoint written in ISO 8601 format

study_inc_temp.name: study_inc_temp
study_inc_temp.title: study incubation temperature
study_inc_temp.description: Sample incubation temperature if unpublished or unvalidated method is used

study_timecourse.name: study_timecourse
study_timecourse.title: time-course duration
study_timecourse.description: For time-course research studies involving samples of the food commodity, indicate the total duration of the time-course study

study_tmnt.name: study_tmnt
study_tmnt.title: study treatment
study_tmnt.description: A process in which the act is intended to modify or alter some other material entity. From the study design, each treatment is comprised of one level of one or multiple factors. This field accepts terms listed under treatment (http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/MCO_0000866). If the proper descriptor is not listed please use text to describe the study treatment. Multiple terms can be separated by one or more pipes

subspecf_gen_lin.name: subspecf_gen_lin
subspecf_gen_lin.title: subspecific genetic lineage
subspecf_gen_lin.description: Information about the genetic distinctness of the sequenced organism below the subspecies level, e.g., serovar, serotype, biotype, ecotype, or any relevant genetic typing schemes like Group I plasmid. Subspecies should not be recorded in this term, but in the NCBI taxonomy. Supply both the lineage name and the lineage rank separated by a colon, e.g., biovar:abc123

substructure_type.name: substructure_type
